programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Two: What's My Name? - Part Two
programme.desc: The boxer refuses induction into the US army as a conscientious objector and becomes one of the most reviled men in America, being stripped of his title, convicted of draft evasion and forced into exile. In 1970, he triumphantly returns to the ring, but it's clear that he has lost a step

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: The Mathematicians pit their wits against the Wordsmiths, trying to draw together the connections between things that at first glance seem utterly random. Hosted by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Critic Robert Hughes assesses how the first European immigrants' religious beliefs influenced American culture, from Spanish Catholic icons in New Mexico to the New England Puritans' stern and severe lifestyles

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that burned to the ground and was preserved in peat. Archaeologists also investigate the cause of the fire. Was it just a terrible accident, or did the villagers' wealth provoke an attack?

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: History Boys v Rugby Boys: Third Heat
programme.desc: A trio of history enthusiasts pit their wits against three Welshmen devoted to their national game of rugby union, making connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Presented by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: Artsnight
programme.desc: In rarely seen interviews writer Carla Lane discusses her life and work, while her son Carl offers personal insights into his mother's career and legacy. With contributions from the Liver Birds - Polly James and Nerys Hughes - and Geoffrey Palmer, the long-suffering husband to Ria in Butterflies

programme.title: Butterflies
programme.desc: Middle-aged housewife Ria worries that life is passing her by and wonders if things will ever perk up. However, she is consumed with guilt on meeting new love interest Leonard, a lonely executive with time on his hands. First episode of Carla Lane's comedy, starring Wendy Craig and Geoffrey Palmer. First aired in 1978

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian investigates the changing role of women in the Victorian era, looking at the work of novelist Elizabeth Gaskell, whose fiction highlighted the plight of needy factory workers. Simon also examines the ground-breaking efforts of doctor Elizabeth Garrett Anderson, health campaigner Mary Seacole and political activists Harriet Stuart Mill and Annie Besant

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round One: The Greatest - Part One
programme.desc: Ken Burns' documentary about the sporting legend and activist, born Cassius Clay in Louisville, Kentucky, in 1942. The first instalment looks at his rise in the world of amateur boxing to win gold at the 1960 Olympic Games, after which he turned professional and moved to Miami to train with Angelo Dundee, sharpening his boxing skills and honing his genius for self-promotion

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round One: The Greatest - Part Two
programme.desc: A look at how the boxer, then known as Cassius Clay, set his sights on becoming the youngest ever world heavyweight champion, taking on the supposedly unbeatable Sonny Liston. He needed to fight other contenders first, including a famous bout with Henry Cooper in London. Meanwhile, he quietly joins the Nation of Islam and becomes a confidant of Malcolm X

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Two: What's My Name? - Part One
programme.desc: After publicly joining the Nation of Islam, Cassius Clay adopts the name Muhammad Ali and dominates the heavyweight ranks for three years, but in 1967 the US army extends the military draft and Ali becomes eligible for service in Vietnam

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: The government orders a massive cut in funds at the university and Stephen tries to save his job as his colleagues rush to impress the assessment team who hold their future in their hands. Surreal drama, starring Peter Davison and Geoffrey Beevers

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: History Boys v Rugby Boys: Third Heat
programme.desc: A trio of history enthusiasts pit their wits against three Welshmen devoted to their national game of rugby union, making connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Presented by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: Leonora Carrington: The Lost Surrealist
programme.desc: Profile of the English surrealist artist, who was a key figure in the movement's heyday in 1930s Paris. Following her evolution from British debutante to artist in exile, living out her days in Mexico City, and exploring her dark and cinematic world

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Charity Puzzlers: Fourth Heat
programme.desc: A trio of chess players apply strategic thinking to challenge their opponents - puzzle writers who use their skills to raise money for their local hospice. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the quiz show

programme.title: Little Ships: The Miracle of Dunkirk
programme.desc: Dan Snow tells the story of the boats that played their part in lifting thousands of Allied troops off the beaches of Dunkirk in the week straddling late May and early June, 1940. Meeting some of the owners, he finds out what happened to the 700-strong flotilla of trawlers, pleasure craft and lifeboats and follows preparations to mark the 70th anniversary of the event as 50 of the surviving vessels return to France

programme.title: The Hadron Collider: In Search of the Peace Particle
programme.desc: A look at the human stories behind mankind's biggest experiment in particle physics. In the aftermath of Hiroshima, a group of scientists vowed never to turn knowledge into weapons again, and from that promise, CERN was born. Blending poetry, music and rare archival footage of Oppenheimer with access to the Large Hadron Collider, this programme explores how science, politics and culture collide

programme.title: Too Near the Sun
programme.desc: The history of the super-bomb, following the race by scientists to imitate the nuclear reactions of the sun and to build the first thermonuclear weapon. Also, exploring how the careers and reputations of some of the scientists involved were seared in the process. First broadcast June 9, 1966

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: The doctors batten down the hatches and put their love lives on hold as an STD epidemic sweeps the campus, and try to locate the Casanova responsible. Peter Davison and David Troughton star in the surreal university drama

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: Stephen and Jock unite to protect an eccentric female lecturer who stands in the way of the Vice-Chancellor's megalomaniac plans, little suspecting she knows a dark secret from his lecherous past. Peter Davison and Graham Crowden star in the surreal university drama

programme.title: The Hadron Collider: In Search of the Peace Particle
programme.desc: A look at the human stories behind mankind's biggest experiment in particle physics. In the aftermath of Hiroshima, a group of scientists vowed never to turn knowledge into weapons again, and from that promise, CERN was born. Blending poetry, music and rare archival footage of Oppenheimer with access to the Large Hadron Collider, this programme explores how science, politics and culture collide

programme.title: Little Ships: The Miracle of Dunkirk
programme.desc: Dan Snow tells the story of the boats that played their part in lifting thousands of Allied troops off the beaches of Dunkirk in the week straddling late May and early June, 1940. Meeting some of the owners, he finds out what happened to the 700-strong flotilla of trawlers, pleasure craft and lifeboats and follows preparations to mark the 70th anniversary of the event as 50 of the surviving vessels return to France

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes celebrity quiz. Krishnan Guru-Murthy, the Rev Kate Bottley, Cherry Healey and Babatunde Aleshen must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for the charity of their choice with each correct answer

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright hears from a man who started getting deliveries that he didn't order - only to discover someone else was intercepting parcels on his doorstep pretending to be him. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan has tips on avoiding a simple doorstep scam and there's advice on what viewers should do if confronted with a burglar in their home

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a man in Devon who is trading his gym membership for the manual labour of property development. Jacqui Joseph is off the beaten path in Norfolk and Dion Dublin finds himself behind bars in Birkenhead

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine presents from Naseby Antiques Fair, Northamptonshire, where experts Gary Pe and Tim Weeks offer up their expertise to assist two teams as they search for profitable items to take to auction. Roo also gets special access to the Royal Crown Derby porcelain factory to learn about centuries of craftsmanship

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie's training switches to care for the elderly, bringing back memories of her beloved dad. In college, the students get hands-on with different aspects of support and try out glasses that simulate conditions like glaucoma, along with weighted suits that affect movement and balance. Natalie's placement takes her to a Royal British Legion care home, where the focus is on keeping the residents active and involved. The programme also meets a group of men who gather as part of the Men's Shed community programme in Newtownabbey

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson joins singer KT Tunstall in her home territory of Fife as they explore the beaches of her childhood near St Andrews, followed by a sea shanty workshop and jewellery making

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps Peggy leave London for a rural Gloucestershire home with views for her, two dogs and horse. Away from the search, Nicki meets a pair of street food chefs who have swapped the city buzz for a slice of country living

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Amelia Bouquet transform a bland and unsafe patch in Barnsley into a boho-style garden influenced by the owners' love of upcycling, crafting and all things rustic

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Ross Kemp in which contestants compete for cash by crossing a bridge made up of stepping stones, each of which has a different option. Friends Mohsin, Gus, Amir and Ayoub must try to get from A to B by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies in order to cross the studio floor safely, or risk falling off the bridge and out of the game

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Ria Lina

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian and Kathy arrive home in good spirits as she tells him how proud she is that he's been voted Councillor Beale, but the pair are unaware of events that are unfolding. Their happiness is short-lived when they receive a knock at the door, and Ian realises there is a chance he could stand to lose everything. Elsehwere, Max drops a bombshell which leaves Cindy shocked

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and the semi-finalists head to the stadium of Premier League football club Brighton and Hove Albion to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Brighton fan Fatboy Slim and players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby pop in to see how they're getting on, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by designer Shayne Brady to assess the final results

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Strictly dancer Amy Dowden turns detective, travelling to west Wales to investigate a rumour of murder in her family. She is shocked to discover that an ancestor died when she was just 14 and investigates how it happened on a farm in 1888. On her dad's side, Amy wants to uncover what led to her grandfather being the only one adopted out of six children. The cause of death of her great grandmother also comes as a painful shock to Amy and she learns about a tale of bravery and hardship in a working-class mining family

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Niall has managed to build a new life, only for old ties to resurface when Mona reconnects with Ruben. Before long events begin to spiral toward an intense and devastating conclusion. Hard-hitting drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell, Amy Manson and Neve McIntosh

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Cheryl turns doorbell detective when she discovers a dodgy duo taking her child's bike from the garden, and tam up with other victims in the hope of finding the bicycle thieves. Former inspector Anton Sullivan heads to the countryside to highlight rural crime, and James confronts the fly-tippers captured by his cameras

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Derbyshire to meet a couple determined to connect a characterful cottage with a beautiful garden to create their dream home. In Tyneside, Martel Maxwell is reacquainted with a developer who always offers inspirational advice. Tommy Walsh is in Stoke-on-Trent, where he is charmed by a house full of Victorian period features

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie learns about dementia care, which proves to be one of the most emotional weeks of her course so far. She takes part in an immersive experience designed to disorientate and confuse - giving a sense of what it's like to have the disease - and talks to her agent Scott, who was married to her former EastEnders colleague Barbara Windsor, and how he looked after her as the disease took over. Finally she puts her learning into practice on a special placement at the Royal British Legion's Poppy Lodge dementia unit

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson and brother David head to a farm near Hexham for a lesson in jam-making. They try making a terrarium - a miniature garden in glass - before heading to their childhood favourite beach, Druridge Bay. There, they help build a gigantic sandcastle complete with turrets

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple to find a home in north Somerset where they can start a family. He also learns about the art of inner calm on a trip to the Somerset Levels

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock revisits some of her favourite gardens the team have renovated. Featuring a jungle garden in Chester, a Scottish and Australian inspired plot in Cambourne and a tranquil space in Petersfield that has a touch of oriental flair

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Ross Kemp in which contestants compete for cash by crossing a bridge made up of stepping stones, each of which has a different option. Friends Jake, Maria, Louise and Hannah must try to get from A to B by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies in order to cross the studio floor safely, or risk falling off the bridge and out of the game

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Vernon Kay present the magazine show, with celebrity guests and stories of interest from around the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Denise attempts to be strong for Chelsea, she hides her own emotions and decides to keep her diagnosis a secret from her loved ones. Yolande covers for her when questions about her previous whereabouts arise. Max's emotions force him into a reckless decision, and George makes a worrying discovery

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The finalists jet off to the Caribbean where they are set three challenges designed to broaden their culinary horizons like never before. For starters, they prepare a barbecue feast for locals, before having a shot at local fine dining at the Estate house within the five-star Jumby Bay Island Resort, with each of them preparing a dish from the dinner menu. They finish off with another task at Jumby Bay - working as private chefs for a VIP dinner, where guests include cricketing legend Viv Richards

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda tries to persuade Felicity to give her the money she needs to buy her dream house. Meanwhile, Fi buys some wheels for her business, which transforms her into a white-van man. Comedy, starring Lucy Punch, Joanna Lumley and Rochenda Sandall

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken is looking forward to having Beth over for dinner, but not before he has painted over the damp patch on the living room ceiling. Richard just wants to read his book in peace, but when Emily and Tim turn up too, an unexpected party gets underway. As the night progresses, secrets threaten to spill out

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl meets a young woman rebuilding her life after being the victim of a violent crime. In an extraordinary turn of events, she embarks on a journey to meet the man responsible, who is now in prison as a result of his actions, so she can ask him the questions that have been brewing since he changed her life. Carl also meets a man who ended up in Maghaberry Prison after funding his gambling addiction by controlling prostitution

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Things do not go to plan when Pip gives evidence in court. Connor takes part in a risky set-up in the hope of extracting information from one of Pip's old foes - and while it doesn't reveal anything about the search for Jamie it does turn up something unexpected about Cara, leading to a heated confrontation between her and Pip

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A sinister message sends Pip, Ravi and Connor to a remote location where a disturbing piece of evidence reveals they are retracing Jamie's steps. An unexpected encounter leaves Pip questioning whether she was right to discount Max as her original suspect. Max shocks everyone with his testimony in court, throwing the proceedings into further uncertainty

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Helen George, Ria Lina, Dean McCullough, Josh Widdicombe, Sam Quek and Sam Campbell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of a Reliant Robin to investigate the reasons behind the lack of three-wheeled cars on the market. The Stig takes the Bentley Continental Supersports for a spin around the test track, and James May fires up the Toyota Hilux for a final challenge - a gruelling drive up Eyjafjallajokull, an active Icelandic volcano that halted flights in British airspace in 2010

programme.title: Brickies
programme.desc: Documentary following young bricklayers on a housing development in Stoke-on-Trent. Jeorgia, from Wales, hopes to realise her dream of buying a sporty new car, but is dealing with the stress of working away from her home and family for the first time in her life. New apprentice Tom T hopes to prove himself as he gets to grips with life in the real working world, while labourer Tom C unexpectedly finds himself without a home and looking for somewhere to live

programme.title: Brickies
programme.desc: The bricklayers are back in Stoke-on-Trent, where things are falling behind schedule and a national mortar shortage threatens to grind production to a halt. Local brickie Patch has been drafted in to give the build an extra push. A young father-of-two, Patch has earmarked a forthcoming family holiday for the moment he hopes to finally 'pop the question' to his long-term girlfriend

programme.title: Brickies
programme.desc: Charismatic newcomer Molly tries to cope on a big site and come out of her comfort zone. The apprentice brickies develop a rivalry, and house-hunting Kuda receives unexpected news

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Young offender Michaella McCollum, who became infamous worldwide as one half of the Peru Two, offers her account of how she came to be arrested, tried and jailed in one of the most notorious maximum security prisons in Peru. She recounts how she and fellow would-be mule Melissa Reid were tasked with pretending to be tourists at Machu Picchu, before walking 12kg of cocaine into Jorge Chávez International Airport in August 2013

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers Steve and Stu scramble to put on a last-minute birthday show for Steve's daughter. If they fail, Steve's in the doghouse and Stu might end up dead. Comic short film, written by and starring Stuart McPherson and Stephen Buchanan

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: One-off comedy about teenage twins from a middle class British Nigerian family who are determined to make their mark on the world. But that proves easier said then done when living with their parents - and even worse, in Milton Keynes. Starring Moses Alexander and Francesca Amewudah-Rivers

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wales v Ghana (Kick-off 7.45pm). All the action from the friendly at Cardiff City Stadium. Wales agonisingly missed out on a place in the FIFA World Cup finals following a penalty shootout defeat at the hands of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the play-offs and are now looking to rebuild. Ghana, on the other hand, did manage to qualify and they are preparing for group matches with England, Croatia and Panama in Canada and the USA

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: The case of a Somerset high-flyer who turned his charm and connections into tools of deception, conning his friends and hundreds of others, while living a life of glamour himself. Through the voices of his victims and event industry insiders, this documentary uncovers the high personal and financial cost of chasing exclusivity, and how easily even the most cautious can be deceived

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.sub-title: Top Gear Africa Special - Part One
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special edition of the motoring show, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Africa on a mission to find the definitive source of the Nile, the world's longest river, which spans more than 4,000 miles. The trio believe they can do a better job than explorers who claim to have discovered the waterway's starting point by travelling further and faster than previous efforts - but the only tools at their disposal are determination, ingenuity and three ageing estate cars

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Uncovering Rape Culture
programme.desc: The reality TV star and model investigates sexism and 'rape culture' in Britain. The former Love Island star uses her own sexual assault at school as a starting point, revealing how it left her with many unanswered questions. She also speaks to other young women about their experiences of sexual assault in school, finding out what action was taken and considering what needs to change

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Animals that live in Asia's deserts and dry grasslands, including the Gobi bear, one of the rarest animals on Earth who communicate by leaving their scent on ancient trees. The Thar in India is the most densely populated desert in the world, and a place where Demoiselle cranes migrate thousands of miles to reach, being welcomed with grain put out by the local Jain community

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and the semi-finalists head to the stadium of Premier League football club Brighton and Hove Albion to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Brighton fan Fatboy Slim and players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby pop in to see how they're getting on, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by designer Shayne Brady to assess the final results

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Things do not go to plan when Pip gives evidence in court. Connor takes part in a risky set-up in the hope of extracting information from one of Pip's old foes - and while it doesn't reveal anything about the search for Jamie it does turn up something unexpected about Cara, leading to a heated confrontation between her and Pip

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A sinister message sends Pip, Ravi and Connor to a remote location where a disturbing piece of evidence reveals they are retracing Jamie's steps. An unexpected encounter leaves Pip questioning whether she was right to discount Max as her original suspect. Max shocks everyone with his testimony in court, throwing the proceedings into further uncertainty

programme.title: Escape Room (2019)
programme.desc: The prospect of winning a significant sum of money lures a group of individuals into a life-threatening scenario. Being locked in several rooms with extreme conditions, they discover the secrets behind the escape room and must fight to survive by finding a way out. Psychological thriller, starring Deborah Ann Woll, Taylor Russell and Tyler Labine

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having previously embarrassing himself in a bar, Liam announces to his girlfriend Jess that he's taking a month off the booze. As he tries to make it through his first sober Friday night in years, he thinks back to where and when his relationship with drinking began as he and his mates tried every trick in the book to buy alcohol

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: After a bad day at work, Liam talks to Jess and is forced to confront his mental health problems, leading him to remember the conflicting advice of two of his old teachers. At school he found little emotional support from PE teacher Mr Stones, but unconventional substitute teacher Miss Monroe made him try to channel his anger into more creative pursuits

programme.title: Dubai Hustle
programme.desc: Brits making it in the world of luxury high-end property in Dubai. Adam and Jake try to impress an influential client and new boy Eddie attempts to become the exclusive agent for a millionaire investor. Chris is under scrutiny

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.sub-title: Top Gear Africa Special - Part Two
programme.desc: After conquering traffic, dangerous mud slides and dubious map reading, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May conclude their quest to find the definitive source of the River Nile in Africa. The trio use a small raft to cross crocodile-infested waters and progress to Lake Victoria, where they hope to find a boat that will take them to the vast plains of the Serengeti. Then, after hundreds of punishing miles in their tired estate cars, the petrolheads make one final push to home in on their target

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: A group of aspiring make-up artists attempt to prove their potential to industry professionals as they battle to be named Britain's next make-up star. Leomie Anderson hosts as 10 new make-up artists create looks for fashion designer HARRI's avant-garde runway show under the watchful eye of key MUA Isamaya Ffrench. Back at Glow Up Studios, influencer Patricia Bright tasks the artists with creating a look inspired by their most influential characteristic

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants dive into the digital world when they create looks for global messaging app Snapchat and content creator Atarah Mayhew. Under the expert eye of Snapchat's head of UK creative strategy Kate Mellett, and Atarah, they are tasked to turn out a bold look under the theme of 'happy place' that could be transformed into an AR lens. For the Creative Brief, they are challenged to 'serve realness' and create a drag look that embodies their truest, most authentic self, with drag star Courtney Act helping Val Garland and Dominic Skinner judge their efforts. Hosted by Leomie Anderson

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Six months after returning home from the adventure of a lifetime, all five teams reunite for the very first time. Together, they look back on their extraordinary 12,000km journey from Palermo in Sicily to the remote wilderness of Hatgal in northern Mongolia, reliving the defining moments of a race that pushed them all to their limits. From the euphoric highs to the brutal setbacks, this celebration of their journey combines unseen footage with exclusive behind-the-scenes access

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan return with the wildlife show, with their new base being at the National Trust's Crom estate on the shores of Lough Erne in Co Fermanagh, with remote cameras rigged across the site to uncover wildlife stories unfolding in real time. Iolo Williams undertakes his own wildlife wanderings, broadcasting live from the Knepp Estate in Sussex, where wildlife is thriving and stand-out species are returning to the landscape.

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian is in the saddle at Wetherby Racecourse, where a team of twins take on a mum-and-daughter duo, with experts Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell by their sides

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey, Michael Portillo arrives in the historic city of Eger. At its castle, he discovers how Ottoman invaders were defeated by an outnumbered Hungarian force. Nearby, in the foothills of the Bükk Mountains, Michael takes a tour of a family owned vineyard. Continuing east, his final destination is the Puszta, Hungary's largest national park

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Mud Rescue, Farm Arson and Drones That Save Lives
programme.desc: In Leicestershire, farmers and a specialist fire and rescue team race to rescue an elderly horse stuck in mud. Arson devastates a family farm in Lincolnshire, destroying buildings, animal enclosures and years of hard work. As the owners count the cost of the damage, another farmer in Scotland recounts watching her tractor explode into flames after a petrol bomb attack. In Hampshire, reporter Paul Colgan meets the innovators using drones to deliver defibrillators to the most remote corners of the countryside

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists join forces with the freedivers to find two family heirlooms. Glass artist Peter turns old thrown away bottles into stained-glass beauty, and archaeologist Sam looks into what could be our oldest find by our youngest treasure hunter

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, who answer questions on the specialist subjects of Marcus Trescothick, Rupert Brooke, Thomas Cromwell, and the films of Wes Anderson, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team has just one day to help Jan, who works seven days a week, transform her bedroom into a relaxing sanctuary and give her space to rediscover her love of fashion

programme.title: The Great British Countryside
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury explore Yorkshire, from the Moors and Dales to the coast. The comedian takes the controls of a glider and gets winched down into one of Britain's largest potholes. Meanwhile, Julia discovers how the county's rocks have turned Agatha Christie's handbag to stone, and reveals how they produce mineral water

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roving team visits Ramsgate in Kent where it finds a valuable collection of cat pictures and makes some interesting discoveries, including a handbag made from an armadillo shell and two teddy bears with their own X-ray plates. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques at Blackpool Tower Circus, where expert James Lewis discovers terracotta tomb figures that could be more than 1,000 years old and Anita Manning assesses a collection of seaside postcards. Paul explores the Morecambe Winter Gardens and tries his hand at some watercolour painting with renowned artist Geoff Butterwort

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch speaking region of Flanders. In Antwerp, he discovers the city's historic multi-billion-pound diamond industry and drops in at the workshop of an expert. Heading to Belgium's third city Ghent, he visits the cathedral and marvels at a 15th-century Van Eyck masterpiece, the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus is on a mission to discover the French equivalent of British fish and chips, while uncovering the secrets of the versatile Provençal olive

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the annual series charting the fortunes of British wildlife during the changing of the seasons

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended edition of the comedy panel show, with Aisling Bea, Roisin Conaty and Jessica Fostekew joining regular Alan Davies to answer questions on senses

programme.title: If Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: Local colleagues, friends and family members come together to tell the stories of Newtownabbey's Mossley Mill and how it has endured. From former mill workers, to local anglers and actors, the mill plays host to a wide range of activities, giving people somewhere to belong and cherish

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl heads to the North Coast, where probation officer Stuart introduces him to a man who has recently been released from Magilligan Prison, but has nowhere to live. Also, a former probationer overcomes her past issues with drugs to accept her university degree, making a pledge to help other people in the same situation

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The Road to Hope with Fergal Keane
programme.desc: BBC journalist Fergal Keane on why he is still full of hope despite witnessing the worst that humanity can do - genocide, war, and famine. The answer lies in the bravery he's encountered in the worst places. Fergal argues that despite the anxiety of the present, the world has real cause for hope

programme.title: Defiance (2008)
programme.desc: Three Jewish brothers escape into the woods during the 1939 Nazi invasion of Poland, and launch a series of guerrilla attacks on German forces. As word of their actions spreads, other refugees join them to form a larger resistance movement. However, one of the brothers fears their struggle is doomed. Second World War drama, starring Daniel Craig, Liev Schreiber and Jamie Bell

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals kick off at the National Railway Museum in York, where the contestants cater for a banquet to celebrate the 200th anniversary of the birth of the modern railway. But fine-dining on this scale, for 100 guests, is beyond anything the amateurs have ever faced before - and to add to the pressure, the menu has been designed by judge Anna Haugh, who is running the service

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian explores how the battle for American independence was not fought only on American soil, but also unfolded in Britain itself, with sabotage, espionage, and political unrest reshaping the empire. She reflects on a revolution that created an independent America and also reshaped Britain and its monarch

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan continue to celebrate the season at National Trust Crom in Northern Ireland. There is a look at a multitude of species in the waterways, revealing a hidden world playing out in the reed beds. Iolo Williams reveals some of the Knepp Estate's most secretive animals and meets the people passionate about rewilding this wildlife haven

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons drain all of their patient's blood from his body to replace damaged arteries. A patient's fertility is at risk without a complex operation to remove diseased bowel

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple who have lived in Stoke-on-Trent for 30 years to relocate to Wiltshire to be close to their grandchild, and learns their secret to a happy marriage

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch speaking region of Flanders. In Antwerp, he discovers the city's historic multi-billion-pound diamond industry and drops in at the workshop of an expert. Heading to Belgium's third city Ghent, he visits the cathedral and marvels at a 15th-century Van Eyck masterpiece, the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo starts a journey in the coastal city of Kanazawa, where he goes behind the scenes with the cleaning detail. At Kanazawa Castle, Michael discovers the history of one of the most important and powerful samurai clans in Japanese history. To finish the day, Michael travels to the Kurobe Gorge railway, a narrow gauge mountain line which snakes along one of Japan's steepest gorges

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey continues in Busan, South Korea's second city, on the country's south eastern coast. On the seafront at Haeundae Beach, he boards the Sky Capsule monorail along a former coastal railway route, reimagined as a scenic ride above the shoreline. Later, he heads to Jeonju, where he meets a craftsman producing paper

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: A Bristol family have a living room over-run with toys, a cluttered kids' bedroom and a utility room that needs organising, so Tommy Walsh and the team see what they can do to help

programme.title: The Great British Countryside
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury visit the South Downs to explore its landscape and geological past. Hugh Dennis learns how the chalk cliffs were formed and why they are such dangerous places for climbing, and also explores a hidden mine. Meanwhile, Julia Bradbury examines the effect of local grassland on the size of horses, and discovers a strange rock that is sharp as steel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team values more decorative objects and rarities when they visit Oban on the west of Scotland. Among the finds is written evidence of a payment for secret services made by George I, as well as furniture from Portugal and a cigarette box from Russia

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Highlights of the best finds by experts Anita Manning, Mark Stacey and others during the programme's travels around the country. programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his Belgian tour, Michael Portillo heads to the beach resort of Knokke-Heist and the world's longest coastal tram, which enjoys views across the North Sea. In the medieval city of Bruges, he visits a saxophone maker and discovers how the instrument, so crucial to the development of jazz, was invented in Belgium

programme.title: Sunshine & Secrets: The Hidden Side of IVF
programme.desc: Northern Cyprus is a sun-soaked haven for affordable IVF. However, behind the glossy clinic websites, parents are discovering their chosen sperm or egg donors might not have been used. This investigation follows parents searching for answers and children wondering where they have come from

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Exploring nuclear fusion - the holy grail for limitless clean energy - and whether it will come soon enough to avoid the worst of the climate crisis

programme.desc: Hannah Fry delves into the mysteries of modern technology to uncover the fascinating stories behind the everyday objects that people take for granted

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.sub-title: UFOs vs the Royal Air Force
programme.desc: The Royal Air Force has been encountering and even engaging UFOs on a regular basic since the Second World War. However, successive governments have continuously denied the sightings. Experts investigate why these UFO facts have been concealed

programme.sub-title: Congressional UFOs, New Nessie and Zombie Fish
programme.desc: An examination of a video recently presented to Congress by the UN Navy that might prove once and for all whether UFOs really exist. Plus, alleged Loch Ness Monster footage that offers the most compelling proof of the creature's existence in years. Marine scientists also discover a mysterious 'Yellow Brick Road' at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team travel around the country assessing items brought in by members of the public, who either take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Huddersfield in West Yorkshire, where Tim Hogarth hopes to take home a zodiac charm bracelet and Helen Gardiner finds an interesting pocket watch. Plus, a commemorative coronation globe gets Stewart Hofgartner in a spin

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A rare Rolling Stones record spins into the shop, but it remains to be seen whether it meets Rick's satisfaction

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A banner from the secret WWII airborne division The Flying Tigers flies into the shop. Plus, Chum gets his hands on a 1980s Ooze-it toy

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling entrepreneur Sarah Moore salvages three skip-bound items from the recycling centre in Witley. To help her give them a new lease of life, Bristol Upholstery Collective gets busy with a box of patchwork pieces, and furniture restyler Emma Walker is challenged with putting her stamp on a dull dressing table. Sarah gives new life to a doll's house in her own project, but will she be able to find new homes for all her items?

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The buyers try their luck at a new facility in La Habra, where Ivy decides to teach them a lesson. Dusty brews himself up an interesting unit, while Kenny weighs the value of childhood memories when selecting a locker

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.sub-title: You Can Lead a Horse to a Locker

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: In Victorville, a friendly duel runs Dusty up, though his mother lode of vinyl might just save him. Ivy's locker shines some light on the business, while Lisa learns that she needs her locker about as much as she needs a hole in her head

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier and Niles throw a singles party, where they're both attracted to the same woman. However, before either of them can make a move, Roz steals the show. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, David Hyde Pierce and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A broken Swiss watch, car trouble, a birthday surprise and a party thrown by Niles's latest lover all combine to ruin Frasier's chances of romance with an attractive new acquaintance. With Kelsey Grammer and Lisa Waltz

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to West Nyack, New York, to come to the aid of a family-run Mexican restaurant that is suffering a financial crisis, and a case of hard-headed patriarchy. When the chef arrives he finds himself turfing out the existing customers for their own safety due to a cockroach infestation on the premises

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Cockermouth, Cumbria, Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole meet blacksmith Jon, who spent many years working on fairgrounds. At the valuation, independent valuer Ronnie Archer-Morgan reckons there's rich pickings to be made from railway memorabilia

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: In France, work is under way to repair the old church at a purpose-built ramshackle Catholic holiday village, and the owners of a hamlet wake up to find it has flooded. In Italy, the owners of an earthquake-damaged borgo find out that caring for their property is not as easy as they thought

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with John Thomson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Liv from Wiltshire has been dreaming of owning a holiday home abroad for many years and, after selling her house in the UK, is finally in the position to make it happen. Ben Hillman helps find some suitable abodes on the west coast of Cyprus within her a budget of £87,000 for Liv and her good friend Daisy to consider

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Roger and Jo cruise to Teddington Lock to recreate their wedding photos. Claire continues her blooming greenhouse tugboat transformation. Michelle heads out of the Lancashire marina for her first proper cruise

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: May and Guy tackle their monumental list of projects at Château de la Vigne. Vivienne and Simon race to complete their Bridgerton-inspired master bedroom at Château de la Croix Boisée. At Château de Lalacelle, Terry and Ashley revamp their chateau's front garden before an open day

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Former chef Liz from Lincolnshire turns to Sharon and Shaun Garner to help her acquire her perfect 'Goldilocks' property in Spain, a country she has had her eye on for 10 years. In Murcia, Darren and Natalie Brown hunt for a dream holiday retreat for recently retired John and his partner Sue. When their first offering misses the mark because of its top floor location, it becomes clear that a large and easily accessible swimming pool for their seven grandchildren is the number one priority

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The teams channel all their patisserie experience into creating 24 identical battenberg cakes and 24 reinvented versions of the colourful classic. They then have five and a half hours to create a dramatic Shakespeare-inspired theatrical scene, complete with puddings based on the 16th-century dessert fruit fool. Ellie Taylor and Liam Charles follow their progress, while Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden taste their efforts

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: Anna feels out of place and struggles to settle back into old routines, seeking guidance from a trusted confidant. David begins to find his feet again, helping out old friends. But as he wrestles with his true feelings, Susan encourages him to take action and face his fears. Romantic drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Paapa Essiedu

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: One of the Queen's Medical Centre's own staff members is admitted after being knocked off her mountain bike. As one of the longest serving members of the A&E team, Lou's colleagues and close friends are concerned she may have serious internal bleeding as well as severe injuries to her shoulder and wrist. Doctors prepare to realign the bones of 57-year-old Stuart, who is in resus with a severely fractured and dislocated ankle, having fallen from a ladder

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Britain's busiest air ambulance crews. In Lanchester, heli-medics struggle to free a man trapped in his mangled car. A tree surgeon falls from height in Ashdon, breaking his femur. A motorbike accident in the Peaks sees medics rushing to the aid of Jamie, who has a seriously damaged arm and other fractures

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox and David Wilson investigate the unsolved murder of Widnes mother-of-two Vera Anderson, who in 1991 answered a mysterious phone call that prompted her to leave home in a hurry. She told her neighbour she would be back in 10 minutes - but five hours later she was found dead in her car, having been strangled and had her throat cut

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Prohibition Grille in Everett, Washington, where he meets belly dancer-turned-restaurateur Rishi Brown, who is making all the wrong moves with her business. Distracted from the task of improving her menu by performing for customers, she's oblivious to the disastrous dishes being served from her own kitchen

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners from Blackpool compete to win the £1,000 prize, including former dancer Rowan and businessman John, who start the proceedings. Also vying to win are Sally and Joanne who hope to impress their guests with an unusual menu that features a Tandoori Tower and a belly-busting breakfast, before competitive duo Holly and Ollie pull out all the stops in an attempt to secure the cash

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows an ambitious build in Cornwall, as Leigh and Richard transform a derelict 17th-century flour mill into a contemporary, three-bedroom home. They employ traditional craftsmen to start work on restoring the exterior, but it's not long before the true scale of the damage to the structure is revealed, and as the costs rise, they are forced to take on more of the work themselves

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.sub-title: George's Tree House and Garden Shed Pub
programme.desc: The architect prepares to unveil his multi-functional treehouse in the grounds of Kielder Water and Forest Park in Northumberland, and meets a couple with big plans to build a theatre in a 1970s caravan. Plus, a man who has created a pub in his garden shed, and a 19th-century shepherd's hut undergoes restoration using Victorian steam-powered machinery

programme.title: Kirstie's House of Craft
programme.desc: Chef Tony Singh and presenter Sophie Morgan compete to make the best holiday snow globes, and TV personality Mark-Francis Vandelli goes head to head with queen of clean Aggie MacKenzie to make intricate jelly art

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Spring arrives and after months of lockdown, the boys are all heading back to school, meaning Graham can get stuck in to project managing the house build. He and Sarah are planning a large dining room for entertaining, with a centrepiece table that used to belong to rock star Ronnie Wood. Artist Graham also draws on his skills to apply some decorative touches to the room - but will it be theatrical enough for Sarah? Meanwhile, the couple also visit a pig farmer who shows them the cycle from piglet to pork chop

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with John Thomson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rebecca rebuffs Sam and tells him that in future she'll only date men who can advance her career. Ted Danson stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: One Happy Chappy in a Snappy Serape
programme.desc: Part two of two. Rebecca faces a dilemma after she is forced to choose her man, and has difficulty deciding whether Martin or Sam will prove to be the lesser of two evils. Bar-room comedy, guest starring Alex Nevil, with Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff, Norm, and Frasier vie for the honour of accompanying Woody to a football game, while Carla convinces herself Eddie is having an affair with a beautiful skater appearing with him in the ice show. American bar-room comedy, starring Rhea Perlman and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ally babysits the twins, and does such a good job that Debra gives her even more responsibility around the house. However, Marie fears her granddaughter is being treated like a slave

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray worries Debra's criticism will make the children think he is a fool, and hatches a plan to win back their respect

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray refuses to take Debra to an awards ceremony, tired of her constant lateness, while Frank and Robert get into their own heated debate about the importance of punctuality. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton, Peter Boyle and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The radio psychiatrist is outraged when Kenny orders him to read adverts for a new sponsor on air, and rallies his fellow presenters to stage a protest. Kelsey Grammer stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The psychiatrist experiences a range of emotions including denial, anger and depression, as he struggles to cope with the pressures of unemployment. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: An invitation to a school reunion drops through Frasier's door - but he is reluctant to attend because he believes the five-yearly event always coincides with a disastrous downturn in his fortunes. Kelsey Grammer stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz suggests that Martin should consider moving in with Niles until Frasier finds a new job as the shrink's unemployed status puts him on edge with everyone

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien continue their mission to repurpose old cast-offs into cash, making an extraordinary find from a Dutch designer in accountant Debbie's lean-to

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: The owners of a ramshackle holiday village advertise for volunteers to complete their renovations and there are worrying power failures at a village in the French countryside

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with John Thomson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth helps Leicestershire couple John and Karly, who have decided to take a leap and follow their dream of buying a holiday home on Spain's Costa Almería that works for them as well as John's four children and eight grandchildren. With a budget of £90,000 to work within, property expert Lee soon some great options for the pair to consider

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Paul and Anthony in Cheshire have an adorable new crewmate. Claire reopens the Shanti Tea Boat on the Aire and Calder Navigation, and Michelle makes great strides with her outreach project in Lancashire

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul take their house apart to replace the very beams that hold the building up. Anna and Max race to finish a courtyard before family descend for a celebratory meal and Terry and Ashley hope their garden will be complete in time for the arrival of 1,000 visitors

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Shaun Garner and dad Steve help Essex couple Christy and Mike find the perfect home for a permanent move to Spain. Darren and Natalie Brown hunt for dream Costa Blanca property for Lincolnshire couple Richard and Sam

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer comes to the aid of Michael - better known as former Olympian Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards - who together with partner Karen is seeking a dream home in Berkshire. The trouble is, they've already viewed 100 houses in 18 months and are getting desperate. Kirstie Allsopp helps first-time buyers Orla and Luke in Hampshire. They've already lost one house they loved but Kirstie knows of another that could be a winner. However, another couple are keen and they are due in 15 minutes, so a speedy viewing is in order

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: As Susan's wedding approaches, David must figure out what it really means to let love in. Anna seeks a fresh start, undeterred by the challenges, and finding support and encouragement from an unlikely source. But as they both embrace change, will their paths bring them together?

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Critiques of shows including 24 Hours in A&E, Five Star Kitchen: Britain's Next Great Chef, Mission: Impossible - Fallout, Sky Coppers, The Chase and Jamie's One-Pan Wonders. Famous faces involved include Rylan and his mother Linda, Guz Khan and Jamali Maddix, Martin and Roman Kemp, Deborah Meaden and Peter Jones, Judi Love and Charlene White, Zoe Ball, Johnny Ball and Woody Cook, Katherine Ryan and her husband Bobby, Miquita and Andi Oliver, Fearne Cotton and Gok Wan, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, and Shaun Ryder and Bez

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Nashville, Tennessee, the spiritual home of country music, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John Chapman's careless methods have damaged his reputation, but he blames the local clientele rather than his less-than-appetising food and disturbing kitchen practices. Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?

programme.title: See No Evil
programme.desc: First of a two-part investigation of the biggest scandal in Church of England history, telling the inside story of prolific abuser John Smyth's sinister world through testimony from his children and widow. In the 1980s, John Smyth was a high-flying barrister offering discipleship to young Christians at Winchester College, then began to invite them to lunches and parties at his family home. Before long he introduced them to a secret and brutal regime, involving cane beatings that he justified as spiritual discipline intended to relieve them of their sins

programme.title: See No Evil
programme.desc: The concluding part explores the new life John Smyth built in Zimbabwe, where he set up Christian summer camps that provided him access to hundreds of young boys every year, with the camps involving enforced nudity, skinny dipping and beatings on a hugely increased scale. The programme also explores the suspicious death of Guide Nyacharu, a schoolboy who was found dead in a swimming pool at one of Smyth's camps

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Matt Shea sets out on the trail of wanted criminals evading justice in an attempt to reveal the shocking scale of this country's hidden fugitive crisis. His journey leads him from safehouses in London to an underground network in Thailand, as he uncovers a shocking new mentality among criminals, where justice is optional. What does this mean for victims of crime and for confidence in the criminal justice system?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Blackpool compete for the £1,000 prize, beginning with practising Buddhist Antonio and former Redcoat Jason, who serve up a sushi starter. A discovery on day two at the home of fireman Daz and his wife Kim raises a few eyebrows, while the final night sees foodies Martin and Kate prepare an authentic Thai menu and a chocolate dessert

programme.title: Kirstie's House of Craft
programme.desc: How to make a tin-can dog and turn the humble loo roll into a host of fun and friendly animals

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Sarah and Graham turn their attention to creating a theatrical dining room, with a large centrepiece table that used to belong to Ronnie Wood

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox and David Wilson investigate the unsolved murder of Widnes mother-of-two Vera Anderson, who in 1991 answered a mysterious phone call that prompted her to leave home in a hurry. She told her neighbour she would be back in 10 minutes - but five hours later she was found dead in her car, having been strangled and had her throat cut

programme.title: Gypsy Rose: Life After Lock Up
programme.desc: Despite the daily online hate she's receiving, Gypsy accepts a public speaking engagement at a university. But after an initial warm reception, more social media trolls get to her

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Handlebar, Casa Roma and The Black Pearl
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he tried to save - Handlebar, Casa Roma and the Black Pearl - to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Yanni's in Seattle, where he clashes with defiant owner Peter Avgoustiou, a chef so set in his ways that he hasn't allowed any changes to his menu since the Greek restaurant opened in 1984. Peter's family is concerned the business is becoming trapped in the past, and standards are slipping, but he seems determined to stick to his guns

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Highlights from the series, focusing on Sarah, her husband Graham and their four boys as they settle into their new rural family life

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Mercedes sees a side to Jenson that she doesn't like, and Myra finds a hidden camera in the house. Things look up for one family, while panic sets in for another, and the police give the Osbornes some worrying news. Two residents decide to move forward together, and Warren steps in to help Mercedes. Friends support Tony as he tries to find the words for Diane's eulogy. Dodger and Theresa take their relationship to the next level, and a resident makes a discovery that stops them in their tracks

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with John Thomson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A desperate Rebecca goes to extraordinary lengths to get a moment alone with Evan Drake as he prepares to move to Japan. Comedy, starring Kirstie Alley and Tom Skerritt

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca is fired and replaced as manager by Sam - who generously rehires her but fails to make it clear there are strings attached. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam finds out an ex-girlfriend's child might be his and swears an oath of celibacy to preclude the possibility of siring any more unwanted heirs. Ted Danson stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The Barones spend Thanksgiving with Amy's parents, but the festivities get off to a bad start by her father's drastic response to the plight of an injured bird. Things liven up when the two families split into teams of Native Americans and Pilgrims to mark the national holiday. Comedy, starring Brad Garrett and Monica Horan

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The kids damage Ray's dictionary, prompting Frank to tell everyone about the time Ray ruined his prized jazz collection. However, he discovers that he may not have been the culprit all along

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra helps to plan a recruitment party at Frank's lodge, leading Ray to grumble about how much time she is spending away from home. Comedy, starring Patricia Hearten and Ray Romano

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles tries to ask Daphne for a date but she misunderstands his intentions, and Frasier has a frustrating episode of his own. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien continue their mission to repurpose old cast-offs into cash, making an extraordinary find from a Dutch designer in accountant Debbie's lean-to

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: The owners of a ramshackle holiday village advertise for volunteers to complete their renovations and there are worrying power failures at a village in the French countryside

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with John Thomson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth helps Leicestershire couple John and Karly, who have decided to take a leap and follow their dream of buying a holiday home on Spain's Costa Almería that works for them as well as John's four children and eight grandchildren. With a budget of £90,000 to work within, property expert Lee soon some great options for the pair to consider

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Paul and Anthony in Cheshire have an adorable new crewmate. Claire reopens the Shanti Tea Boat on the Aire and Calder Navigation, and Michelle makes great strides with her outreach project in Lancashire

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul take their house apart to replace the very beams that hold the building up. Anna and Max race to finish a courtyard before family descend for a celebratory meal and Terry and Ashley hope their garden will be complete in time for the arrival of 1,000 visitors

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Shaun Garner and dad Steve help Essex couple Christy and Mike find the perfect home for a permanent move to Spain. Darren and Natalie Brown hunt for dream Costa Blanca property for Lincolnshire couple Richard and Sam

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer comes to the aid of Michael - better known as former Olympian Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards - who together with partner Karen is seeking a dream home in Berkshire. The trouble is, they've already viewed 100 houses in 18 months and are getting desperate. Kirstie Allsopp helps first-time buyers Orla and Luke in Hampshire. They've already lost one house they loved but Kirstie knows of another that could be a winner. However, another couple are keen and they are due in 15 minutes, so a speedy viewing is in order

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: As Susan's wedding approaches, David must figure out what it really means to let love in. Anna seeks a fresh start, undeterred by the challenges, and finding support and encouragement from an unlikely source. But as they both embrace change, will their paths bring them together?

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Critiques of shows including 24 Hours in A&E, Five Star Kitchen: Britain's Next Great Chef, Mission: Impossible - Fallout, Sky Coppers, The Chase and Jamie's One-Pan Wonders. Famous faces involved include Rylan and his mother Linda, Guz Khan and Jamali Maddix, Martin and Roman Kemp, Deborah Meaden and Peter Jones, Judi Love and Charlene White, Zoe Ball, Johnny Ball and Woody Cook, Katherine Ryan and her husband Bobby, Miquita and Andi Oliver, Fearne Cotton and Gok Wan, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, and Shaun Ryder and Bez

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Britain's busiest air ambulance crews. In Lanchester, heli-medics struggle to free a man trapped in his mangled car. A tree surgeon falls from height in Ashdon, breaking his femur. A motorbike accident in the Peaks sees medics rushing to the aid of Jamie, who has a seriously damaged arm and other fractures

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Drama written by Russell T Davies, about a bar owner in Manchester's gay village who becomes embroiled in a feud with his long-standing neighbour. Leo fights to defend the safety of one of his bar staff, while his next-door neighbour Clive is searching for work, and realises Leo might be the answer to all of his problems. Starring Alan Cumming and David Morrissey

programme.title: See No Evil
programme.desc: The concluding part explores the new life John Smyth built in Zimbabwe, where he set up Christian summer camps that provided him access to hundreds of young boys every year, with the camps involving enforced nudity, skinny dipping and beatings on a hugely increased scale. The programme also explores the suspicious death of Guide Nyacharu, a schoolboy who was found dead in a swimming pool at one of Smyth's camps

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Matt Shea sets out on the trail of wanted criminals evading justice in an attempt to reveal the shocking scale of this country's hidden fugitive crisis. His journey leads him from safehouses in London to an underground network in Thailand, as he uncovers a shocking new mentality among criminals, where justice is optional. What does this mean for victims of crime and for confidence in the criminal justice system?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Blackpool compete for the £1,000 prize, beginning with practising Buddhist Antonio and former Redcoat Jason, who serve up a sushi starter. A discovery on day two at the home of fireman Daz and his wife Kim raises a few eyebrows, while the final night sees foodies Martin and Kate prepare an authentic Thai menu and a chocolate dessert

They then head off for a bike ride - but what Barney forgets to mention to his dad is that they are riding on a tightrope 25 metres in the air

Anna Richardson assists both singletons, inviting them to choose their favourite partner from a line-up of potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time until fully nude.
