programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. Randy's hoarding is out of control, while Alexa, who runs the kitchen, has a lot to learn. To make matters worse, the Old Neighborhood is one of the filthiest eateries the fiery chef has ever seen, and only one of the biggest transformations of the series can save the business

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With Decision Day looming, the couples prepare for the biggest call of their lives, questioning love, trust and authenticity in the process. One wife is blindsided by life-changing news

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley turns 21, so the adults hit a bar, where Claire is desperate to appear more of a pal than a nagging, uptight mum, and Cameron and Mitchell are distressed when their 'cool' credentials come up short. Phil and Jay's quick pitstop to buy a present takes a disastrous turn, too, while back at home, Alex, Manny and Luke are left to babysit Lily

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The Dunphys and Manny are on their way back from breakfast at the pancake house when Phil is forced to swerve out of the way of an oncoming truck, a brush with death that prompts them all to re-evaulate their lives. Comedy, starring Ty Burrell

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An irreverent take on the Prince and the Pauper. Bart enthusiastically trades lives with wealthy lookalike Simon Woosterfield after landing himself in trouble. Simon settles into life at home with the Simpsons, but Bart finds the lavish lifestyle too much to handle when he becomes the target of all his new friends' pranks

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer makes it his mission to find a devastated Smithers a boyfriend and signs him up to a dating app. Lisa gets a leading role in the school production of Casablanca, leaving Milhouse jealous of her co-star and Bart plotting sabotage. Guest starring George Takei

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: How Lisa Got Her Marge Back
programme.desc: Marge takes Lisa on a theatre trip to the big city to make up for a rough patch in their relationship. Bart is frustrated that everyone seems to be well prepared for his pranks in advance, so he teams up with Maggie to play practical jokes on unsuspecting victims

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa takes a trip to the mall to buy a new gadget, while Bart manages to cause chaos. After making a hasty retreat, he befriends a boy named Bashir - but Homer begins to believe that his son's new pal is involved in a terrorist plot

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The squad has time to kill when the arrival of the Serbian president's motorcade to Brooklyn is delayed, so Peralta launches the seventh annual Jimmy Jab Games, an eccentric series of races and contests held in the precinct building. Meanwhile, Jeffords and Holt must set up a new drug task force approved by Holt's nemesis, Madeline Wuntch

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: It's Halloween again, and this time Peralta bets Holt he can steal his treasured watch before midnight, enlisting criminal assistance to achieve his goal. Gina struggles to keep up with her college course, perform a full-time job and attend dance rehearsals - leading to problems both in and out of work

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: There is scepticism, competition and high emotions when Gloria wins a bid at the school auction for a family seminar with famous doctor and author Debra Radcliffe, whose self-help book, Clean Out Your Junk Drawer, encourages people to get out all their emotional junk to become healthier people and better partners and spouses

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Gloria rents a cabin in the mountains in which she hopes she and her family will be able to enjoy a picture-perfect white Christmas. However, when the clan arrives, unhelpful weather and a random eccentric guest conspire to ruin the festive cheer. Meanwhile, Mitch and Cam resolve to make up for their bad carolling experience last year, and Jay has a huge announcement that catches Claire off-guard. Comedy, starring Sofia Vergara

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The American comedy returns after a mid-series break. Jay struggles with the age gap between the parents of Joe's latest play date, while Cam he tries to act as matchmaker between Lily and her crush, only to discover that he's completely misunderstood the direction of her affections. Elsewhere, Claire and Phil attempt to split the bill with cheque-dodging friends on a double date, and Haley, Alex and Luke run into Goodfellas star Ray Liotta while searching for a last-minute birthday surprise for Mitchell

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Fed up with having the ducklings around the house, Claire and the kids hatch a secret plan to help them fly the nest. Meanwhile, Jay helps out Mitchell when he throws a slumber party for Lily's Vietnamese dance troupe and Gloria and Manny are forced to stage a hot pepper heist when a huge sauce order comes in

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge starts her own sandwich shop after her snacks become a huge hit at Springfield Elementary. However, running a business proves challenging - especially when the same franchise opens another location across the street

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Eco-disaster looms in Springfield when Mr Burns launches a controversial fracking operation - and while Homer is more than happy to play accomplice to his boss, an outraged Lisa calls on the services of bigshot politician Maxine Lombard (voiced by two-time Oscar winner Jane Fonda) to shut Burns down. Against the odds, however, the opponents end up falling for one another

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In a crossover episode with Futurama, the Planet Express crew members travel back in time and turn up in Springfield on a mission to prevent the troublesome family from destroying the future. It's not just a question of asking nicely however - Bender has been programmed to kill Bart, Terminator-style. Can the clan foil the murderous bot?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart faces a tough test at school when he gets a bullying new teacher who seems to want to crush his spirit, so the spiky-haired tearaway becomes determined to fight back. Meanwhile, the family attends a hippie-style community festival. With the guest voice of Willem Dafoe

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: A Roman legion is sent on a mission to crush a Pictish uprising, only to be nearly wiped out in a savage ambush. A centurion who survives the massacre takes charge of the survivors and leads them on a daring mission to rescue their captured general and make it back to their own territory alive. Neil Marshall's action thriller, with Michael Fassbender, Dominic West and Olga Kurylenko

programme.title: Escape Plan 3: The Extractors (2019)
programme.desc: After a couple of previous high stakes assignments, security expert Ray Breslin is hired to rescue the kidnapped daughter of a Hong Kong tech mogul from a formidable Latvian prison. Problems continues to arise when Breslin's girlfriend is also captured. Director John Herzfeld's action thriller sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Dave Bautista and 50 Cent

programme.title: Deadly Target (2015)
programme.desc: A soldier returning home from duty overseas witnesses his brother's murder, and vows to get revenge. As he pursues the killers, he uncovers a secret conspiracy within the US government and must fight for survival against a ruthless assassin sent to stop him revealing what he knows. Action thriller, starring Michael Jai White and Steve Austin

programme.desc: The lawman tracks down a hijacked ambulance containing a donor organ. Dionne Warwick guest stars

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Bionic Woman _ Part One
programme.desc: Part one. Steve is understandably shocked to discover his supposedly dead fiancee is actually alive and kicking

programme.desc: A series of devastating earthquakes are evidence of a massive crack opening up in the surface of the planet. A seismologist teams up with an explosives expert to prevent the world being completely destroyed by the quakes. Disaster movie, starring Brittany Murphy, Eriq La Salle and Bruce Davison

programme.title: High Flight (1957)
programme.desc: The Commanding Officer of an RAF Training School must deal with a difficult cadet, whose carefree attitude and refusal to obey orders puts his life at risk. Drama, starring Ray Milland and Bernard Lee

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Night of the Big Heat (1967)
programme.desc: Invaders from outer space take over a remote Scottish island and raise the temperature to an unbearable level, giving residents little or no chance of survival. Sci-fi adventure, starring Christopher Lee, Peter Cushing and Patrick Allen

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk runs into an old enemy who killed his friend years ago and decides to have one more shot at defeating the vampire-like creature. Sci-fi adventure, starring William Shatner

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Bionic Woman _ Part Two
programme.desc: Part two. After failing to restore Jaime's memory, Steve takes her on an anti-terrorist mission - but will it help? Seventies sci-fi adventures, starring Lee Majors and Lindsay Wagner

programme.desc: Stacy and Hooker search for a vice girl who hold the key to posthumously clearing a murdered officer accused of taking bribes. Eighties police drama, starring William Shatner and Adrian Zmed

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The rangers try to stop a drugs cartel while an promising young boxer receives counsel from Walker. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Carlito's Way (1993)
programme.desc: A former gangster is released from jail and vows to go straight, hoping to earn enough money to escape his New York roots - but his misguided loyalty to a crooked lawyer threatens to drag him back into a life of crime. Brian De Palma's thriller, starring Al Pacino, Sean Penn, Penelope Ann Miller, John Leguizamo and Viggo Mortensen

programme.title: Unchained (2017)
programme.desc: Three criminals find themselves trapped in a warehouse with the law closing in, but an even greater threat lurks inside. Thriller, starring Adrien Brody and John Malkovich

programme.title: Curse of the Crimson Altar (1968)
programme.desc: A man goes looking for his missing brother in a country house, and finds it is home to a decadent society. Troubled by strange hallucinations and the warnings of a mysterious professor, he investigates further, and stumbles on a cult worshipping an ancient witch with human sacrifices. Horror, starring Boris Karloff, Christopher Lee, Mark Eden and Barbara Steele

programme.title: Malibu Shark Attack (2009)
programme.desc: An underwater earthquake causes a tsunami that unleashes a pack of goblin sharks on an unsuspecting Malibu beach. Quick thinking from the local lifeguards ensures the area is cleared before the monsters strike, but a number of people remain abandoned in high water and must fight the creatures to reach dry land. Horror, starring Peta Wilson, Warren Christie and Chelan Simmons

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Shalako (1968)
programme.desc: A party of European aristocrats on a hunting trip in New Mexico falls foul of marauding Apaches and the treachery of a guide, who abandons the group and makes off with their possessions. However, a US army scout knows of their presence in the territory and sets out to the rescue. Western, with Sean Connery, Brigitte Bardot, Jack Hawkins, Honor Blackman, Woody Strode, Peter Van Eyck and Eric Sykes

programme.title: Angel and the Bad Man (2009)
programme.desc: Remake of the John Wayne classic telling the story of notorious gunfighter, Quirt Evans. Western, starring Lou Diamond Phillips and Deborah Kara Unger

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk sets Scotty up with a date to help him recover from a recent injury, but doesn't bargain on his friend becoming the centre of a murder investigation. Sci-fi adventure, starring William Shatner and James Doohan

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Plans to take the legendary Liberty Bell on a tour of America are thwarted by an unbalanced scientist threatening to destroy it

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Former partner Jim Cody asks Hooker to help him crack a drug-related murder, only to find the case has unfortunate personal repercussions. William Shatner and Jim Brown star

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Four female inmate escape from a prison bus and two of them return to robbing banks. Meanwhile, Alex discovers one of the women had the wrong legal representation during her trial

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: The Fan (1996)
programme.desc: An unbalanced baseball fan takes hero worship to extremes while attempting to help a player get over a recent slump - but it is not long before the sports star realises his new friend is seriously disturbed. Tony Scott's thriller, starring Robert De Niro, Wesley Snipes, Ellen Barkin and John Leguizamo

programme.title: Best of the Best
programme.desc: A martial arts team is assembled to represent America in an international taekwondo tournament in South Korea, but conflict within the squad threatens their chances of victory. Martial arts drama, starring Eric Roberts, James Earl Jones, Phillip Rhee and Sally Kirkland.

programme.title: POW: The Escape (1986)
programme.desc: As the Vietnam War comes to an end, one man embarks on a life-threatening mission to free American prisoners held in a secret Vietcong camp. Action, starring David Carradine

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a cafe worker struggling to speak, having possibly suffered a stroke. A woman who's in the process of giving birth to her seventh child hasn't felt the baby move in over 10 hours and there are signs the baby could be in distress. Another pregnant woman and her toddler son are involved in a car crash, and a patient is found collapsed in a car park

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew fight to save their patient's partially amputated hand in Stowmarket and a crew in High Wycombe to help a man in cardiac arrest. The Essex and Herts Air Ambulance team deal with Becky's badly broken ankle in Chelmsford and in Hatfield, a motorcyclist suffers multiple injuries after a collision with a van

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. Porter Chris, who's been part of the A&E department for 25 years, shares his insights into the job as he goes about his day

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: With their tour of the Highlands drawing to a close, the campers head for Scotland's northern coast, pitching up at Barrow Campsite near Nairn. Having visited five campsites, it's time for Sean and Marie, Simon and Emmy, and Ian, Jen and Ellis to pick this week's perfect pitch. But first - there's some paddleboarding, beach yoga and campfire cocktails to enjoy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire and Worcestershire. Henry makes a table from the base of a broken agricultural grain mill, while Simon creates a garden seat from an old mangle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team visits Swindon and Chippenham, where Henry Cole finds and restores a much-maligned BSA Ariel 3 moped and Simon O'Brien gives a Ford Transit gearbox a new lease of life

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jon and Julia Shorrock host the first round of the B&B competition from their four-star Derwent House in West Sussex. The guests are split by gender to sample the area, with the men firing machine guns at a shooting range and the women relaxing at a spa - but a carpeted bathroom and shower without hot water raise questions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Peter and Sharron Sorsky invite their fellow competitors to Kirkby Park B&B in Liverpool, but the presence of a security camera on the outside of the house leaves one guest feeling uneasy. Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive B&B Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Annette McAnespie welcomes the group to Hazelwood Farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, for the final B&B visit. The initial impressions are favourable and Peter and Jon begin to bond for the first time during a mosaic class, but not everyone feels up to the planned activity of a cycling tour

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespie's farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the week's winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nursing manager Sarah hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, taking inspiration from her husband's Middle Eastern heritage to offer her guests a Turkish fusion menu. Joining her is 28-year-old painter decorator Alex, 45-year-old bridal shop owner Abbi, 60-year-old community centre manager Janet and 56-year-old record label boss Phil. A hilarious evening lies in store, with unexpected tattoos, beauty tips and twerking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Would-be clairvoyant Janet hosts the second of a week of dinner parties in Leeds and hopes her ghoul-themed dinner party will help her win the cash prize. Part of Janet's plan is to pretend her house is haunted and she has a practical joke lined up for nursing manager Sarah and bridal shop owner Abbi to find. With more surprises in store, will the fright-night theme be enough to ghost Janet into the lead?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Record label owner Phil hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, hoping to win the £1,000 prize money by serving up a 1970s rock-themed menu. His guests are astounded by his mystery past, and painter and decorator Alex is unimpressed when wedding shop owner Abbi continues to wind him up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Painter and decorator Alex hosts the fourth night in Leeds, hoping to win the prize money by serving up a cruise-themed menu from around the world - which impresses jealous rival Janet so much, she almost turns violent. Abbi continues to belittle the host - until he reveals he spends his evenings on stage impersonating a famous heart-throb singer. Will his performance bag him the top prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bridal shop owner Abbi hopes to woo her guests with a wedding-themed party on the final day of the competition in Leeds. All week Abbi and Michael Buble impersonator Alex have had a bit of a banter that has sailed close to the wind. Will the tension take over, or can the wedding theme create an amorous atmosphere that will help Abbi win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Four sets of campsite proprietors battle it out to be named best value, with the competition beginning at Amber's Bell Tent Camping in Mannington, Norfolk, a retro-styled pre-erected campsite where artistic host Amber Wykes challenges her guests to go native with a series of bushcraft trials. Over dinner, guests Derek and Julie's opinions on glamping irritate both Amber, and glampsite owners Nick and Sheila. Then back at the tents, brother-and-sister team Russell and Jane aren't too impressed with the sleeping arrangements. In the morning, the self-catering breakfast leads to complaints aimed squarely at Amber

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second day of the competition is at Park Farm Camping and Caravan Site in Norfolk, where husband and wife team Derek and Julie Nice are proud to show off their budget, family-friendly site. Former marksman Derek treats his guests to a spot of clay pigeon shooting, bringing out his competitive side

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to 500-year-old Layer Marney Tower in Essex for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed into luxury glamping lodges by Nick and Sheila Charrington. The guests have little to complain about with the accommodation, but a revelation over dinner changes how they see things. Plus, a treasure hunt organised by the hosts sends one team on a wild goose chase

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Day four is at the Foxhunter Park in Kent, where brother and sister Jane Stacey and Russell Saunders own a caravan site that includes a bistro and entertainment. The accommodation is luxurious but the large scale of the site does not sit well with the campers - and the evening entertainment of a fire-eating limbo dancer leaves most of them cold

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The campers meet for the final time to find out what they thought one another's stay was worth. Amber is offended by Derek's low scoring, while he is thrown by Nick and Sheila's feedback. Russell and Jane have an axe to grind with Derek whose low scoring of their park is made worse by what they see as a personal attack on their hosting, while Nick and Sheila appear to emerge relatively unscathed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In Bath, Kirstie Allsopp meets first time buyers Laura and Phil, who are desperate to make the move from shared living to a home of their own. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer goes property searching with downsizing duo Kerrie and Dave, who sold their beloved barn conversion to move closer to their kids

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the third heat, as music enthusiasts from across the country are put to the test, on their knowledge of everything from one-hit wonders to chart-topping legends. Who can come out top of the charts and secure their place on the leaderboard?

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice's train odyssey takes her from Athens and across the Corinth Canal to Corinth, where she tracks down the precise place where St Paul stood to preach Christianity to the masses on his Greek pilgrimage. From Corinth she heads to Piraeus to meet an expert who has a theory on the value of the ideas of the Ancient Greeks to our modern situation. After learning about sea battles between the Greeks and the Romans, Alice takes a ferry to the historic island of Paros in the Cyclades

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: Myth of the Vampire; Lost Inca Gold; King Arthur's Cradle
programme.desc: Exploring how, in a castle in Transylvania, the legend of a bloodthirsty tyrant lives on - even after his death. This programme also looks at a medieval Inca castle, where a cursed bounty lies buried. Plus, inspecting a castle on a rugged peninsula, which is supposedly the birthplace of England's legendary king

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-three-year-old Mohammad, who has motor neurone disease, is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis and doctors work to get the infection under control, while David has fallen 20 feet from a billboard and the trauma team is concerned that the 47-year-old has suffered life-changing injuries. Retired journalist Tom is in the urgent care centre after knocking his arm and his wife Eileen reveals how their plans have been turned upside down due to an unexpected bombshell six weeks previously

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a cafe worker struggling to speak, having possibly suffered a stroke. A woman who's in the process of giving birth to her seventh child hasn't felt the baby move in over 10 hours and there are signs the baby could be in distress. Another pregnant woman and her toddler son are involved in a car crash, and a patient is found collapsed in a car park

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: This week's camping connoisseurs go in search of the perfect pitch in north Wales. Their first stop is the coastal campsite of Porth Iago on the Llyn Peninsula

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Sheffield, Henry Cole makes good money restoring an antique pine sideboard, but has less success with a pair of old brass jugs. And Simon O'Brien converts a staircase into shelves and makes an eye-watering profit from restoring an optician's desk

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Hampshire and Surrey, where Henry makes a pair of coffee tables from a cable drum and a galvanised water tank, and Simon rescues a winged back chair and upholsters it with his own designer fabric

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The sports car-themed PitStop in Hertfordshire hosts the first round, as owners Melvyn and Sindy Rutter try to make a good impression. However, the absence of tea and coffee in the rooms does not go down well with the guests, and Melvyn's after-dinner talk about his collection of Morgan cars fails to spark much interest

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gill and Peter Tydie invite their fellow competitors to stay at the Victory in Mersea, Essex, a four-star B&B with 26 members of staff to run the bar, restaurant and accommodation. A trip to a local fishery gives one contestant an opportunity to annoy his peers, and the discovery of a hole in a bedsheet leaves the hosts feeling uneasy about their chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave Botha hosts at his Butterfly Paragliding B&B on the Isle of Wight. Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham are unhappy they must sleep in a tepee in the garden and use an outdoor toilet, and claim they were served watery eggs and rubbery sausages for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham host the final night at Braddon Hall in Torquay, Devon, where Melvyn and Sindy take issue with their squeaky four-poster bed. The hoteliers try to entertain their guests by inviting them to take part in an archery contest - which ends in accusations of unfair play

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. One of the hosts gets upset about large underpayments and a couple interrogate their guests about the criticism in their feedback, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Kent, 51-year-old construction site manager and part-time bouncer Mark hopes that his dinner party can be as much about the party as it is about the dinner, as he hosts a cheesy fun-fuelled evening. Will party poppers, balloons, confetti cannons and surprising celebrity stories impress 53-year-old dog-lover Karen, 39-year-old Czech shop assistant Verush, 42-year-old singer Amrick and 58-year-old fashion photographer Anthony?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Kent, competitive sales assistant Verush is going all out to win by hosting a Czech-themed evening, but ex-stripper Mark isn't impressed, comparing Verush's pasta to MDF and pastry to leather, while the first night's argument between dog lover Karen and photographer Anthony is reignited

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog enthusiast Karen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Kent, hoping to impress her guests by serving up a menu inspired by her four-legged friends. After the cheesy fun of the previous night, she is determined to impress her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Amrick takes his turn to host in Kent, hoping to wow his guests by revealing his musical talents and with his traditional Indian cuisine. But with dog-lover Karen and competitive Verush not liking hot and spicy food, there could be trouble - while photographer Anthony questions ex-stripper Mark's dress sense

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Anthony attempts to wow his guests with an evening of fine food and culinary excellence on the final day of the competition in Kent. However, the soiree is jeopardised by ex-stripper Mark on overdrive and dog-lover Karen showcasing some incredible impressions from animals to Anthony himself. The host might be cockily confident he can win, but Karen seemingly remains in pole position to bag the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Swallows Rest in Weymouth, Dorset, where hosts Jane and Keith Smith are pulling out all the stops to win. However, the colour combinations in the rooms do not go down well with rival B&B owner Ray Chowdhury, and an afternoon of drawing starts some competition between Ray and newlyweds James and Darren Kosa-Jones

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the Burley Inn in the New Forest, where newlyweds Darren and James welcome their guests. Donna and David prove to have a critical eye when they discover holes in the sheets, and Jane and Keith have a disagreement over a toilet roll. In the afternoon, the couple reveal their quizmaster skills, before Ray grills them on how they run their B&B over dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Hotel Montville in Redditch, Worcestershire for the third day of the competition, where first impressions of Ray Chowdhury's unique style and decor are positive. However, room inspections leave Jane and Keith Smith threatening not to stay, and the guests grill their host about his business background at dinner. Although some light relief is given in the form of ice sculpting, breakfast disappoints James and Darren Kosa-Jones, and Ray must face the harsh reality of his guests' opinions at feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to Donna and David's Silverton B&B in Shrewsbury, where the complimentary earplugs soon have Darren and James worrying about the noise from the road outside. A Segway race helps whip up the guests' competitive spirit, Donna gets emotional when her work-life balance is questioned, and chef David proves he can handle the unexpected at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Accusations fly around the table as payments are revealed and the winner announced. Swallows Rest is first up and hosts Jane and Keith want to find out about Ray's colour preferences. Next Darren and James of the Burley Inn quiz Ray over his fussiness around their pine furniture. When it comes to the Silverton, B&B team Donna and David take umbrage with Jane and Keith's comments

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jacinta and Marco, who have outgrown their home in Didsbury, Greater Manchester, while Phil Spencer assists newly engaged Olivia and Amy, who want to settle back in the North after five years on the move

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the fourth heat. Relive the hits and see if you really know the charts! Only one PopMaster player can secure their place to play 3 in 10 - will they leave with a silver or gold disc?

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at bold, modern places that challenge the idea of what heritage can be, and the places that can be the easiest to dismiss and the hardest to save. He steps inside Liverpool's Metropolitan Cathedral, which is flooded with coloured light from one of Britain's most spectacular stained-glass lanterns, and visits London Zoo's penguin pool, where the aquatic birds are treated to cutting-edge modernism

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at bold, modern places that challenge the idea of what heritage can be, and the places that can be the easiest to dismiss and the hardest to save. He steps inside Liverpool's Metropolitan Cathedral, which is flooded with coloured light from one of Britain's most spectacular stained-glass lanterns, and visits London Zoo's penguin pool, where the aquatic birds are treated to cutting-edge modernism

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping connoisseurs arrive at Cefn Coed, nestled within an ancient oak forest near the Mawddach Estuary, where highlights include forest bathing and a Thai cooking class

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. Porter Chris, who's been part of the A&E department for 25 years, shares his insights into the job as he goes about his day

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: With their tour of the Highlands drawing to a close, the campers head for Scotland's northern coast, pitching up at Barrow Campsite near Nairn. Having visited five campsites, it's time for Sean and Marie, Simon and Emmy, and Ian, Jen and Ellis to pick this week's perfect pitch. But first - there's some paddleboarding, beach yoga and campfire cocktails to enjoy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire and Worcestershire. Henry makes a table from the base of a broken agricultural grain mill, while Simon creates a garden seat from an old mangle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team visits Swindon and Chippenham, where Henry Cole finds and restores a much-maligned BSA Ariel 3 moped and Simon O'Brien gives a Ford Transit gearbox a new lease of life

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jon and Julia Shorrock host the first round of the B&B competition from their four-star Derwent House in West Sussex. The guests are split by gender to sample the area, with the men firing machine guns at a shooting range and the women relaxing at a spa - but a carpeted bathroom and shower without hot water raise questions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Peter and Sharron Sorsky invite their fellow competitors to Kirkby Park B&B in Liverpool, but the presence of a security camera on the outside of the house leaves one guest feeling uneasy. Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive B&B Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Annette McAnespie welcomes the group to Hazelwood Farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, for the final B&B visit. The initial impressions are favourable and Peter and Jon begin to bond for the first time during a mosaic class, but not everyone feels up to the planned activity of a cycling tour

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespie's farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the week's winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nursing manager Sarah hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, taking inspiration from her husband's Middle Eastern heritage to offer her guests a Turkish fusion menu. Joining her is 28-year-old painter decorator Alex, 45-year-old bridal shop owner Abbi, 60-year-old community centre manager Janet and 56-year-old record label boss Phil. A hilarious evening lies in store, with unexpected tattoos, beauty tips and twerking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Would-be clairvoyant Janet hosts the second of a week of dinner parties in Leeds and hopes her ghoul-themed dinner party will help her win the cash prize. Part of Janet's plan is to pretend her house is haunted and she has a practical joke lined up for nursing manager Sarah and bridal shop owner Abbi to find. With more surprises in store, will the fright-night theme be enough to ghost Janet into the lead?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Record label owner Phil hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, hoping to win the £1,000 prize money by serving up a 1970s rock-themed menu. His guests are astounded by his mystery past, and painter and decorator Alex is unimpressed when wedding shop owner Abbi continues to wind him up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Painter and decorator Alex hosts the fourth night in Leeds, hoping to win the prize money by serving up a cruise-themed menu from around the world - which impresses jealous rival Janet so much, she almost turns violent. Abbi continues to belittle the host - until he reveals he spends his evenings on stage impersonating a famous heart-throb singer. Will his performance bag him the top prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bridal shop owner Abbi hopes to woo her guests with a wedding-themed party on the final day of the competition in Leeds. All week Abbi and Michael Buble impersonator Alex have had a bit of a banter that has sailed close to the wind. Will the tension take over, or can the wedding theme create an amorous atmosphere that will help Abbi win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Four sets of campsite proprietors battle it out to be named best value, with the competition beginning at Amber's Bell Tent Camping in Mannington, Norfolk, a retro-styled pre-erected campsite where artistic host Amber Wykes challenges her guests to go native with a series of bushcraft trials. Over dinner, guests Derek and Julie's opinions on glamping irritate both Amber, and glampsite owners Nick and Sheila. Then back at the tents, brother-and-sister team Russell and Jane aren't too impressed with the sleeping arrangements. In the morning, the self-catering breakfast leads to complaints aimed squarely at Amber

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second day of the competition is at Park Farm Camping and Caravan Site in Norfolk, where husband and wife team Derek and Julie Nice are proud to show off their budget, family-friendly site. Former marksman Derek treats his guests to a spot of clay pigeon shooting, bringing out his competitive side

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to 500-year-old Layer Marney Tower in Essex for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed into luxury glamping lodges by Nick and Sheila Charrington. The guests have little to complain about with the accommodation, but a revelation over dinner changes how they see things. Plus, a treasure hunt organised by the hosts sends one team on a wild goose chase

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Day four is at the Foxhunter Park in Kent, where brother and sister Jane Stacey and Russell Saunders own a caravan site that includes a bistro and entertainment. The accommodation is luxurious but the large scale of the site does not sit well with the campers - and the evening entertainment of a fire-eating limbo dancer leaves most of them cold

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The campers meet for the final time to find out what they thought one another's stay was worth. Amber is offended by Derek's low scoring, while he is thrown by Nick and Sheila's feedback. Russell and Jane have an axe to grind with Derek whose low scoring of their park is made worse by what they see as a personal attack on their hosting, while Nick and Sheila appear to emerge relatively unscathed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In Bath, Kirstie Allsopp meets first time buyers Laura and Phil, who are desperate to make the move from shared living to a home of their own. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer goes property searching with downsizing duo Kerrie and Dave, who sold their beloved barn conversion to move closer to their kids

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the third heat, as music enthusiasts from across the country are put to the test, on their knowledge of everything from one-hit wonders to chart-topping legends. Who can come out top of the charts and secure their place on the leaderboard?

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice's train odyssey takes her from Athens and across the Corinth Canal to Corinth, where she tracks down the precise place where St Paul stood to preach Christianity to the masses on his Greek pilgrimage. From Corinth she heads to Piraeus to meet an expert who has a theory on the value of the ideas of the Ancient Greeks to our modern situation. After learning about sea battles between the Greeks and the Romans, Alice takes a ferry to the historic island of Paros in the Cyclades

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: Myth of the Vampire; Lost Inca Gold; King Arthur's Cradle
programme.desc: Exploring how, in a castle in Transylvania, the legend of a bloodthirsty tyrant lives on - even after his death. This programme also looks at a medieval Inca castle, where a cursed bounty lies buried. Plus, inspecting a castle on a rugged peninsula, which is supposedly the birthplace of England's legendary king

Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A two-year-old is having a constant seizure and needs to get to hospital urgently; he won't stop fitting and his oxygen levels are falling. programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow Kathleen Fraser Jackson's search for her mother, with the 62-year-old having been trying to track down the woman who gave her up for adoption for more than 40 years. programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Alice Jones was brought up by her father, but on a visit to her mother more than 50 years ago she met her younger brother called Sam and has always hoped that one day she would see him again. Mark Ratcliffe grew up in Lancashire and at the age of 12 his parents told him he was adopted and the revelation devastated him. programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian, presenter and actor Matt Lucas had an exceptionally close bond with his late grandmother, Margot, who came to the UK in 1939 as a Jewish refugee from Nazi Germany. Matt has never fully understood what Margot experienced as a young woman in Berlin in the lead up to the Second World War - or what became of the extended family she left behind. Matt's poignant journey takes him to Berlin, and then to Amsterdam where he discovers an extraordinary connection to Anne Frank, as well as the fate of his many family members who had fled to Holland, which was later was invaded by the Germans

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Izzie Balmer hunt for profit-making antiques in Snowdonia. Scent bottles, brick moulds, and cloisonne bowls attract the attention of these antiques aficionados. programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow Kathleen Fraser Jackson's search for her mother, with the 62-year-old having been trying to track down the woman who gave her up for adoption for more than 40 years. In a last-ditch attempt to help Kathleen find her mum, the Long Lost Family team samples Kathleen's DNA and adds it to an online database, which triggers an epic journey spanning continents and cultures, and unlocking more secrets - and family members - than she could ever have dreamed of

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Alice Jones was brought up by her father, but on a visit to her mother more than 50 years ago she met her younger brother called Sam and has always hoped that one day she would see him again. Mark Ratcliffe grew up in Lancashire and at the age of 12 his parents told him he was adopted and the revelation devastated him. programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian, presenter and actor Matt Lucas had an exceptionally close bond with his late grandmother, Margot, who came to the UK in 1939 as a Jewish refugee from Nazi Germany. Matt has never fully understood what Margot experienced as a young woman in Berlin in the lead up to the Second World War - or what became of the extended family she left behind. Matt's poignant journey takes him to Berlin, and then to Amsterdam where he discovers an extraordinary connection to Anne Frank, as well as the fate of his many family members who had fled to Holland, which was later was invaded by the Germans

programme.desc: A thick fog traps Cheese and Egg, his gang, and their inspector at the depot, forcing them to spend the night together in the canteen and navigate an uneasy coexistence.

programme.title: Second Thoughts
programme.desc: Hannah steps into the breach as Bill's escort when Faith falls ill just before his magazine's dinner, but she can't find a thing to wear. Comedy, starring Lynda Bellingham, James Bolam and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: The New Statesman
programme.desc: Alan puts on the charm for Margaret Thatcher's secretary, and makes a lunchtime date when he discovers the Iron Lady's right-hand woman is rather hot stuff. Meanwhile, Piers is set to marry the attractive and dominant Clarissa, and asks B'stard to be his best man

programme.title: The New Statesman
programme.desc: Piers gets promoted and an enraged Alan videotapes Sarah as she carries on an affair with their gardener, then files for divorce on grounds of adultery. Comedy, starring Rik Mayall and Marsha Fitzalan

programme.title: Sex with the Stars (1980)
programme.desc: A shy astrology columnist finds his life thrown into chaos when a sex-obsessed American takes over as editor and sets him an unusual assignment. He is ordered to sleep with 12 different women, one for each star sign, and write an article about each one. Comedy, starring Martin Burrows and Thick Wilson

programme.title: Horror Hospital (1973)
programme.desc: An aspiring rock star is persuaded to take a relaxing break at a health farm, but gets a rude awakening on discovering his fellow guests all have identical scars on their foreheads. Horror, starring Michael Gough, Robin Askwith, Dennis Price and Vanessa Shaw

programme.title: Flesh for Frankenstein (1973)
programme.desc: An insane baron is determined to create a master race, constructing male and female creatures from assorted body parts. Meanwhile his sex-starved wife takes an interest in a stable boy, unaware her new lover is merely there to rescue his missing friend, whose brain has been used to complete the male monster. Horror, with Udo Kier, Joe Dallesandro, Monique Van Vooren, and Arno Juerging

programme.title: Stereo Underground
programme.desc: Richard Latto presents alternative anthems from a music community with attitude.

programme.title: The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie
programme.desc: With famous ballerina Anna Pavlova due to appear in Edinburgh. the eccentric teacher insists her girls experience this important cultural event, and enthusiastically predicts the future stardom of one of her students. Starring Geraldine McEwan

programme.title: The New Statesman
programme.desc: Alan puts on the charm for Margaret Thatcher's secretary, and makes a lunchtime date when he discovers the Iron Lady's right-hand woman is rather hot stuff. Meanwhile, Piers is set to marry the attractive and dominant Clarissa, and asks B'stard to be his best man

programme.title: The New Statesman
programme.desc: Piers gets promoted and an enraged Alan videotapes Sarah as she carries on an affair with their gardener, then files for divorce on grounds of adultery. Comedy, starring Rik Mayall and Marsha Fitzalan

programme.title: Stereo Underground
programme.desc: Richard Latto presents alternative anthems from a music community with attitude.

programme.title: Jack Hargreaves' Old Country
programme.desc: Jack celebrates rural England, blending folklore, crafts, and gentle storytelling as he explores vanishing traditions, landscapes, and the quiet wisdom of country life.

programme.title: Rewind Time Machine
programme.desc: Aficionados gather to celebrate real ale in 1975.

programme.title: Discovering Gardens
programme.desc: Gyles Brandreth and Michele Brown explore stunning public gardens across South West England in this fun, informative series-perfect for beginners and expert gardeners alike.

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: If You Say It Out Loud, It's Real
programme.desc: Hurt by Anna's absence, Rachel becomes even more unsettled when Claire accidentally lets something slip. Maggie receives big news of her own and later crosses paths with Luke, who is still shaken by his last encounter with Rachel. At the flat, Anna is caught off guard by an unexpected visit from Janie, who arrives with startling news

programme.title: Joan
programme.desc: Aiming to provide a stable home for Kelly by outdoing her foster parents financially. Joan joins Boisie in stealing a painting by George Stubbs, but the plan goes awry when the owner's ex-husband unexpectedly appears. Angry about how Boisie handles it, Joan storms out, but an impending visit by social services forces her to reconcile with him

programme.title: Cheap Irish Homes
programme.desc: Maggie Molloy and Tadhg Casey help husband and wife Kieran and Rebekah Moore search for a character home on a plot of around one acre in Counties Leitrim or Cavan. The couple are keen to take on a project but would also be happy viewing a property that needed less work

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian and Kathy arrive home in good spirits as she tells him how proud she is that he's been voted Councillor Beale, but the pair are unaware of events that are unfolding. Their happiness is short-lived when they receive a knock at the door, and Ian realises there is a chance he could stand to lose everything. Elsehwere, Max drops a bombshell which leaves Cindy shocked

programme.title: Fair City
programme.desc: Hayley, James and Holly are aghast at Emma's harsh new house rules, and tensions grow when Max lands a jibe about Dean's age. A guilty Orla lets Pete take the fall for a bad garage review, and Jacinta delivers an ultimatum that could force Fergal back under her roof, and her thumb

programme.title: Ear to the Ground
programme.desc: Darragh McCullough visits a Robotic Dairy system in Meath, Stephen Robb meets tillage farmer Martin Ennis in north county Dublin, and Ella McSweeney visits Charles Carr on his farm in Galway

programme.title: Wild Cherry
programme.desc: In the town of Richford Lake, things are perfect for supermum Juliet and business mogul Lorna. But when their teenage daughters Allegra and Grace are accused by their school of filming a provocative video, this idyllic town falls to pieces. Drama, starring Eve Best and Carme Ejogo

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie cares for Arlean Brewer, a heavily pregnant unmarried woman recently discharged from the Linchmere Mental Hospital after being diagnosed with depression and mania. When Arlene struggles with her new psychiatric drug regime, Trixie must draw on all her medical and emotional skills to avert disaster. Rising rates of gonorrhoea in the district cause alarm, and Dr Turner decides to implement a contact-tracing scheme run by the Greater London Council. Rosalind and Cyril volunteer at a homeless shelter

programme.title: Clean Sweep
programme.desc: Jason reminds Shelly to attend the police station for fingerprinting and DNA testing - so she goes to extreme lengths to change make sure her records won't match. Glencara houskeeper Eileen gives a description of the woman seen leaving the dead man's room, while the investigation in London is developing new leads - despite political pressure to close the case

programme.title: The Kelly Clarkson Show
programme.desc: Kelly is joined by guests Willie Geist, Luke Kirby and Gideon Glick

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Sloan's birthday turns out to be less than festive when he discovers the dead body of a prominent divorce lawyer in the hospital pharmacy. When Steve arrives, however, the evidence is nowhere to be found. Mark and Jack work feverishly to recover the missing body, find the culprit, and lead a tour of potential investors at the same time. Dick Van Dyke stars as the medical sleuth

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris in the Outer Hebrides, beginning with a visit to a nuclear power station on the Hunterston Peninsula. He then learns about Largs' Viking past and admires one of the most beautiful railway stations in Britain at Wemyss Bay. The importance of ferries to connect western Scotland with its many islands becomes plain to Michael at Port Glasgow in the deep waters of the Clyde, where he visits Ferguson Marine, builders of Calmac ferries

programme.title: RTÉ News: One O'Clock and Weather
programme.desc: National and international news

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie demands to know why Tane broke up with Jo, Sonny suggests he move back in with Dana, but she doesn't feel it's possible

programme.title: Shortland Street
programme.desc: A lonely Desi seeks comfort, Vili struggles to make amends, and the pressure gets to Harper

programme.title: Fair City
programme.desc: Hayley, James and Holly are aghast at Emma's harsh new house rules, and tensions grow when Max lands a jibe about Dean's age. A guilty Orla lets Pete take the fall for a bad garage review, and Jacinta delivers an ultimatum that could force Fergal back under her roof, and her thumb

programme.title: Ireland's Fittest Family
programme.desc: Coach Donncha O'Callaghan has the Hickey family from Dublin, the Linehan family from Cork, and coach Andrew Trimble has the McKenna family from Monaghan, and the Hilliard family from Kildare. They all battle it out for one of the three places up for grabs in the next round of the competition at Hell and Back

programme.desc: The contestants are thrown into their first big Service Challenge! With Jamie Oliver running the kitchen, they must feed 40 returning MasterChef Legends with a three-course meal

programme.desc: The latest news headlines

programme.title: RTÉ News
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: The Angelus
programme.desc: Horologist Maurice Keenan, at work in his Carrick-on-Shannon workshop, carries on the skills learned from his late father, in the process creating a meditation on time

programme.title: RTÉ News: Six One and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international headlines

programme.title: Nationwide
programme.desc: Featuring two women who are trailblazers in the world of sport, education and medicine. Presented by Bláthnaid Ní Chofaigh

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Denise attempts to be strong for Chelsea, she hides her own emotions and decides to keep her diagnosis a secret from her loved ones. Yolande covers for her when questions about her previous whereabouts arise. Max's emotions force him into a reckless decision, and George makes a worrying discovery

programme.title: Donal's Real Time Recipes
programme.desc: Donal Skehan cooks duck breast served over a rich cassoulet, built from tins and pulses, and a fiery twist on a classic with spicy 'nduja carbonara. The chef also makes cacio e pepe orzo with chicken

programme.title: Neven's English Food Tour
programme.desc: Neven Maguire travels through the North East of England, visiting the region's culinary gems, tasting a variety of different dishes, and meeting the chefs behind them. In Newcastle, he meets Terry Laybourne, the first chef in Newcastle to be awarded a Michelin Star. Neven enjoys bar snacks at Terry's pub, The Broad Chare, including scotch eggs made with haggis

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine O'Clock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Uncharted with Ray Goggins
programme.desc: Singer Keith Duffy is venturing to Uganda with his actor son Jay for the test of a lifetime. Father and son will attempt to summit one of Africa's highest and least climbed peaks in the Rwenzori Mountains alongside Ray Goggins. It's a long and gruelling ascent of seven days through knee deep bog and alpine rock to its glacier peak, making it one of the continents toughest expeditions

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: As Maigret reels from regret, he suffers a blow in his personal life. Maigret digs into Fumal's life in order to understand him better

programme.title: Swindlers
programme.sub-title: Custom House Capital: A House of Cards
programme.desc: The Custom House Capital scandal, one of Ireland's most notorious investment frauds, which involved the systematic misappropriation of approximately 61 million euros in client funds. The fallout left more than 2,000 investors, many of whom were retirees, facing devastating financial losses. This documentary allows many of them to tell their stories about how their trust was breached, leaving them facing retirement with just a fraction of their life savings

programme.title: Tommy Sands: Clann an Cheoil
programme.desc: Documentary insight into one of Ireland's most successful and influential folk singer-songwriters, and how he and his family have acquired an international legacy as musicians, social activists and storytellers. Tommy and his family, Anne, Colum and Ben, have always been immersed in music and storytelling, their home a haven for Catholics and Protestants to come together to share tunes and tales. The film takes viewers back to where it all started and hears from those who have been influenced by the Sands family, including Arlo Gurhrie

programme.title: Welcome To Moore Street
programme.desc: Featuring a glimpse into the world of delivery riders in the Brazilian bike shop and learning about plans to restore the National Monument on the street. Young drag queen Noah performs on Moore Street during the Pride festival, while Georgian seamstress Liza celebrates one year in business

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Young couple Sean and Jess O'Tuama from County Limerick are restoring a parochial house in rural County Limerick to its former glory. They plan to do some major structural repairs, tear apart the inside, reveal the original stonework and design their own kitchen and bedrooms. When the couple start peeling back layers of old plasterwork they realise that they have more work on their hands than they envisaged

programme.title: Kerry: Tides of Time
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of a single year, as the seasons play out through and across the county's varied habitats. This programme looks in detail at the lives of some of the area's most charismatic and fascinating wild inhabitants. Badgers, red deer, hen harrier and puffins are among the cast of characters whose lives are followed through the year

programme.title: Clean Sweep
programme.desc: Jason reminds Shelly to attend the police station for fingerprinting and DNA testing - so she goes to extreme lengths to change make sure her records won't match. Glencara houskeeper Eileen gives a description of the woman seen leaving the dead man's room, while the investigation in London is developing new leads - despite political pressure to close the case

programme.desc: As Denise attempts to be strong for Chelsea, she hides her own emotions and decides to keep her diagnosis a secret from her loved ones. Yolande covers for her when questions about her previous whereabouts arise. Max's emotions force him into a reckless decision, and George makes a worrying discovery

programme.title: Donal's Real Time Recipes
programme.desc: Donal Skehan cooks duck breast served over a rich cassoulet, built from tins and pulses, and a fiery twist on a classic with spicy 'nduja carbonara. The chef also makes cacio e pepe orzo with chicken

programme.title: Neven's English Food Tour
programme.desc: Neven Maguire travels through the North East of England, visiting the region's culinary gems, tasting a variety of different dishes, and meeting the chefs behind them. In Newcastle, he meets Terry Laybourne, the first chef in Newcastle to be awarded a Michelin Star. Neven enjoys bar snacks at Terry's pub, The Broad Chare, including scotch eggs made with haggis

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine O'Clock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Uncharted with Ray Goggins
programme.desc: Singer Keith Duffy is venturing to Uganda with his actor son Jay for the test of a lifetime. Father and son will attempt to summit one of Africa's highest and least climbed peaks in the Rwenzori Mountains alongside Ray Goggins. It's a long and gruelling ascent of seven days through knee deep bog and alpine rock to its glacier peak, making it one of the continents toughest expeditions

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: As Maigret reels from regret, he suffers a blow in his personal life. Maigret digs into Fumal's life in order to understand him better

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie cares for Arlean Brewer, a heavily pregnant unmarried woman recently discharged from the Linchmere Mental Hospital after being diagnosed with depression and mania. When Arlene struggles with her new psychiatric drug regime, Trixie must draw on all her medical and emotional skills to avert disaster. Rising rates of gonorrhoea in the district cause alarm, and Dr Turner decides to implement a contact-tracing scheme run by the Greater London Council. Rosalind and Cyril volunteer at a homeless shelter

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Ember is forced to confess to her family that human bone fragments were found in the boat hut, but Hannah can't believe her dad was responsible and pleads with Ember to look further. When a new development propels Ember into dangerous territory, the truth is finally revealed, and it proves far more shocking than she ever expected

programme.title: Tommy Sands: Clann an Cheoil
programme.desc: Documentary insight into one of Ireland's most successful and influential folk singer-songwriters, and how he and his family have acquired an international legacy as musicians, social activists and storytellers. Tommy and his family, Anne, Colum and Ben, have always been immersed in music and storytelling, their home a haven for Catholics and Protestants to come together to share tunes and tales. The film takes viewers back to where it all started and hears from those who have been influenced by the Sands family, including Arlo Gurhrie

programme.title: Welcome To Moore Street
programme.desc: Featuring a glimpse into the world of delivery riders in the Brazilian bike shop and learning about plans to restore the National Monument on the street. Young drag queen Noah performs on Moore Street during the Pride festival, while Georgian seamstress Liza celebrates one year in business

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Young couple Sean and Jess O'Tuama from County Limerick are restoring a parochial house in rural County Limerick to its former glory. They plan to do some major structural repairs, tear apart the inside, reveal the original stonework and design their own kitchen and bedrooms. When the couple start peeling back layers of old plasterwork they realise that they have more work on their hands than they envisaged

programme.title: Kerry: Tides of Time
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of a single year, as the seasons play out through and across the county's varied habitats. This programme looks in detail at the lives of some of the area's most charismatic and fascinating wild inhabitants. Badgers, red deer, hen harrier and puffins are among the cast of characters whose lives are followed through the year

programme.title: An Ghig Mhór
programme.desc: Donegal singer-songwriter Eve Belle helps fellow Donegal musician Hannah B from Gaoth Dobhair to organise their first big gig. In Irish

programme.title: Changing Ends
programme.desc: A vicious stray dog forces the Carrs to have some unexpected quality time together, but the front room starts to resemble a pressure cooker with Nige, Ange and Nanna Joyce trapped in the house with them too. A little white lie of Alan's causes confrontations and frustrations, and he soon realises that honesty is not always the best policy when it comes to family affairs. Comedy, starring Oliver Savell

programme.title: Mammoth
programme.desc: PE teacher Tony Mammoth was presumed dead in an avalanche in 1979, but is brought back to life in the present day and gets his old job back, approaching it exactly the same way as he did in the 1970s. Unaware of developments, Nick reveals that he and Melissa have started sleeping together again - which upsets Frances further as she reflects on how quickly things have deteriorated. The next morning Bobbi reveals that Melissa has shown her Frances's short story. Bobbi is furious with both the content of the story and Frances' dishonesty. Hurt by Frances too many times, she ends the friendship. Drama, starring Alison Oliver, Joe Alwyn, Sasha Lane and Jemima Kirke

programme.title: Daddy Issues
programme.desc: Gemma receives dodgy life advice from her incarcerated sister Catherine and Rita, her alcoholic boss. Worried that she will be having the baby alone, she enlists Cherry to help her find a suitable, ideally rich, father for her unborn child. Meanwhile, Malcolm thinks he has found love with a woman online. Gemma educates him on the perils of catfishing - but Derek encourages Malcolm to meet her. Comedy, starring Aimee Lou Wood, David Morrissey and Taj Atwal

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Catherine Fox continues to wreak havoc, threatening to fire all of the interns until Dr Miranda Bailey intervenes and puts her own position on the line. Elsewhere, Meredith licks her wounds after Catherine's attack on her research with Dr Amelia Shepherd. A ghost from the past unsettles one of the staff and doctors are presented with unique injuries after a series of accidents at a climate change protest

programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: Mulholland Drive (2001)
programme.desc: An actress arrives home to find a mysterious woman in her room who is suffering from amnesia. She sets out with the stranger to discover how she got there - but the trail quickly leads to intrigue, blackmail and murder. David Lynch's mystery, starring Naomi Watts, Justin Theroux, Laura Harring and Robert Forster

programme.title: Kiss Me, Kate (1958)
programme.desc: A divorced husband-and-wife acting duo's constant bickering threatens to disrupts a stage production of Shakespeare's The Taming of the Shrew in which they are the romantic leads. Meanwhile, a case of mistaken identity attracts backstage interlopers in the form of two gangland thugs looking to collect a gambling debt. Adaptation of Cole Porter's Broadway musical comedy, with Alfred Drake and Patricia Morison

programme.title: Dickens in Italy with David Harewood
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actor and writer follows in the footsteps of Charles Dickens' little-known journey through Italy's most beautiful citie. David uncovers Italy's influence on the author's writing style and political beliefs as he traces the novelist's Grand Tour through Venice, Naples, Genoa and Rome

programme.title: Painting Birds with Jim and Nancy Moir
programme.desc: Jim and Nancy head to Lancashire looking for the cute but elusive bearded tit, and are joined by broadcaster Chris Packham, local experts, birders and artists

programme.title: John Williams: The Berlin Concert
programme.desc: The composer conducts the Berliner Philharmoniker in a wide-ranging selection of music from some of his best-known film scores

programme.title: Watercolour Challenge
programme.desc: Hannah Gordon presents the art competition in which amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man is haunted by an unhappy childhood and taunted by school bullies - and a stranger on a train could be responsible for his misery. Drama, starring John Mills and Anthony Steel

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Rev George Duckworth has problems attracting female attention, but things change when a beautiful woman joins his congregation. Drama, starring Joan Collins and John Alderton

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Even though he has several large bank accounts, miserly Alexander Gifford is so obsessed with money that he begins to see his wife as nothing but a collection of expenses. When she finds his hidden accounts, the trouble really unfolds. With Dennis Day and Alice Backes

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A writer interested in a lucrative story makes an arrangement to spend the night in a murderers' den, an area of a wax museum dedicated to infamous killers. But he doesn't get the story he expected. With Barry Nelson and Everett Sloane

programme.title: Discovering Film

programme.desc: The worlds created by authors of classic children's literature, revealing much about their characters and the times they lived in

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A couple rely on home-made medicine to treat their sickly child, using royal jelly from the family's bee hives. However, the outcome proves frightening. Drama, starring Susan George and Timothy West

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A deadly snake strikes in the heat of Kashmir, creeping into a young teacher's bed and settling on his belly. Drama, with Judy Geeson, Andrew Ray, Anthony Steel and Saeed Jaffrey

programme.desc: Three parolled safecrackers plan to heist a fortune in jewels, but inadvertently steal a container of radioactive cobalt. Drama, with James Gregory, Chris Robinson and Norman Fell

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Musicians and rock historians discuss the making of Cream's groundbreaking 1967 album Disraeli Gears. Recorded in just six days at Atlantic Records' studios in New York, it was produced by Felix Pappalardi and set the seal on the band's fame

programme.desc: A profile of the English actor, who found fame in TV drama Brideshead Revisited before starring in films including The Mission, and Reversal of Fortune, for which he won an Oscar
