programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: When a driver picks up a hitch-hiker in his car, his mundane journey turns into a thrilling ride involving cunning trickery. Drama, starring Rod Taylor and Cyril Cusack

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents

programme.desc: Revisit the career of Mary Cassatt through prints, pastels and paintings, exploring the often-overlooked impressionist's influence on how women appear in art

programme.title: Klimt and The Kiss
programme.desc: Experts examine Gustav Klimt's early 20th-century painting The Kiss and establish just what makes it such an enduring, endearing piece

programme.desc: A dynamic portrait of Scottish writer Irvine Welsh explores his creative journey through DJing, drug experiences, and literary work.

programme.desc: Axe turns to Chuck for a favour involving a common adversary, while Prince manoeuvres to raise his public profile. Chuck seeks counsel from an out-of-the-box business mind. Wendy works to run employee feedback rounds, even at the cost of Prince's pride

programme.title: We Own This City
programme.desc: Maurice Ward recounts how Jenkins remained unscathed despite a series of illegal incidents. Baltimore is rocked by the Freddie Gray protests. Crime drama, starring Jon Bernthal

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: On election night, Tom tries to manage an increasingly chaotic newsroom, while the Roy siblings spar over the outcome. Starring Jeremy Strong, Sarah Snook and Kieran Culkin

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Prince clashes with his employees over who has ultimate authority, while Ira turns to Chuck for help with an embarrassing problem. Wendy and Luke forge a connection and Sacker confronts a figure from her past who poses a threat to her future

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Separated and under pressure, Amber turns to Tibor for help as a wider threat begins to emerge. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Ponies
programme.desc: As the widows begin their training, Bea becomes the handler for Soviet technician Sasha and catches the eye of dangerous KGB officer Andrei. Meanwhile, Twila's assignment entertaining a businessman uncovers a much deeper conspiracy

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: After being enlisted for the arduous task of archiving material from Deborah's entire career, Ava gains invaluable insight into her employer. An unexpected package fuels longstanding tensions between Deborah and an estranged family member, and later, Ava reaches out to someone from her own past

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: As Deborah scrambles to keep her dates intact, an unexpected tip drops in her lap at a bat mitzvah. Meanwhile, after DJ's request for a favor from Deborah is rejected, Ava accompanies her to a jewellery expo - only to learn more about Deborah and DJ's complicated mother-daughter relationship

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: While her retreat with Deborah isn't what she envisioned it provides Ava a chance to correct a mistake. Finding himself with rare time off, Marcus reaches out to a surprising source for company. Later, Deborah reveals the truth about a major myth in her career

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.sub-title: Strangers and Other Partners _ Part Two
programme.desc: Part two. Mike and Julianna face the light of day after their night of passion. Reed Diamond stars

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part one of three. A body is found in a hotel's toilets during a black tie function that Gee attends

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores the history of Bristol, beginning by learning a grisly secret at the docks before meeting Mercury Prize-winning musician Roni Size to discuss life in the St Pauls and Stokes Croft areas of the city. He also explores the district of Clifton and visits a speakeasy-style bar

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. Among his destinations are a 1930s banking building that has remained unaltered since closing in 2007, a well-appointed set of toilets and a recording studio hidden in a bar. He also discovers which former dictator once spent Christmas in the city

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Maritime Inn landlady Pat gets her first full house of the season as Brixham's tourist trade picks up for the summer - but refuses to hire anyone to help her with the extra work. Powerboat instructor Mike Weatherbee tries to enforce the laws of the sea, carpet-cleaner Dave Satchwill prepares to lead the town's popular ghost tour, and lone fisherman Dave Brown reveals his unusual methods

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: August arrives and the fishermen of Brixham spend time ashore with their families as their boats are repaired - but summer proves costly for Jan, who loses £1,000 for every day his vessel is out of action. Apprentice deckhand Logan has to decide whether a life at sea is the right choice for him and his family, and seafood seller Jenny receives a surprise on her 65th birthday

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part two of three. The investigation into the murder of Felix Wilson's housekeeper continues

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part three of three. The detectives are divided as the investigation into the murder of Felix Wilson's employee continues, and racial tensions are brought to the fore

programme.title: Hacks

programme.title: Catherine the Great
programme.desc: Catherine struggles to deal with Potemkin's moods and jealousy and tragedy strikes for Paul, forcing her to resort to extreme measures

programme.title: Billions

programme.title: We Own This City

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: Amid rising tensions, Shiv tries to reposition herself within a new political landscape, while Kendall rallies supporters. Jeremy Strong, Sarah Snook and Kieran Culkin star

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: As a yacht party extends into the night and friends explore Bangkok's clubs, parallel stories unfold with family disagreements over future plans and mysterious happenings at a nearby hotel

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Hacks

After a disagreement with Marcus over the future of her career, Deborah reveals her cards to Marty

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: While DJ shows off her new man at her birthday party, Deborah and Marty put their differences aside to enjoy each other's company. However, when the celebration becomes a surprise dual function, Ava is tasked with an important responsibility

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: After Marty delivers a crushing blow, Deborah and Ava travel to a comedy club in Sacramento to try out her new material, which inadvertently sparks a career-defining moment for Deborah. Meanwhile, Ava finds herself stuck between her parents' competing interests, and Marcus takes a step forward with Wilson. Comedy drama, starring Jean Smart, Hannah Einbinder and Carl Clemons-Hopkins

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: As George fights to protect his public image, Agnes' status quo is disrupted and Armstrong intensifies her campaign against Peggy

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part two of three. The investigation into the murder of Felix Wilson's housekeeper continues

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part three of three. The detectives are divided as the investigation into the murder of Felix Wilson's employee continues, and racial tensions are brought to the fore

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the world's most luxurious hotels and begins in Miami's South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatra's regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: The Blues Brothers (1980)
programme.desc: Two criminal brothers discover the orphanage where they were raised faces closure unless the nuns who run it can pay their tax bill, so the pair embark on an urgent mission to revive their old blues band to raise the money needed. Comedy, starring John Belushi, Dan Aykroyd, Aretha Franklin, James Brown, Ray Charles, Cab Calloway, Carrie Fisher, John Candy and John Lee Hooker

programme.title: Blazing Saddles (1974)
programme.desc: A crooked railway tycoon whose men are terrorising a frontier town arranges for a black sheriff to be appointed, hoping the racist residents will reject him. The lawman proves more cunning than expected, and with the help of a drunken gunslinger, runs rings around the businessman. Mel Brooks' Western spoof, in which he co-stars alongside Cleavon Little, Gene Wilder and Madeline Kahn

programme.title: Eddington (2025)
programme.desc: A stand-off between a small-town sheriff and mayor sparks a powder keg as neighbours are pitted against each other in Eddington, New Mexico. Modern-day Western, starring Joaquin Phoenix, Emma Stone and Pedro Pascal

programme.title: The Man with Two Brains (1983)
programme.desc: A brilliant surgeon escapes the heartache and frustration of his failing marriage by falling in love with a bottled human brain - but needs to find a suitable body to implant it in. Sci-fi comedy, starring Steve Martin, Kathleen Turner and David Warner, with Sissy Spacek providing the voice of the doctor's beloved brain

programme.title: Caddyshack (1980)
programme.desc: A host of weird and wacky characters cause mayhem at an exclusive golf club, including a laid-back professional player, an obnoxious millionaire, the snobbish club president, a motley collection of teenage caddies and a psychotic groundskeeper engaging in a fight to the death with a canny gopher. Comedy, starring Chevy Chase, Bill Murray, Michael O'Keefe and Rodney Dangerfield

programme.title: National Lampoon's Vacation (1983)
programme.desc: A bumbling all-American family man takes his wife and kids on a disaster-filled cross-country drive to California to visit famous theme park Walley World - but their chances of arriving at their destination in one piece are looking slim. Comedy, starring Chevy Chase, Beverly D'Angelo, Randy Quaid, Imogene Coca, Anthony Michael Hall, Christie Brinkley and John Candy

programme.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles (1987)
programme.desc: A stressed businessman just wants to make it home to spend Thanksgiving with his family. However, when his flight is forced to land miles from its destination, he finds himself sharing a disaster-strewn journey across America with an amiable but slobbish salesman. Comedy road movie, starring Steve Martin, John Candy, Laila Robins, Kevin Bacon and Michael McKean

programme.title: The Breakfast Club (1985)
programme.desc: Five teenagers who occupy different areas of their school's social structure are thrown together for a Saturday morning detention. During the day, they analyse their behaviour and find unexpected common ground. Drama, directed by John Hughes and starring Emilio Estevez, Judd Nelson, Molly Ringwald, Anthony Michael Hall, Ally Sheedy and Paul Gleason

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes' sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Mars Attacks! (1996)
programme.desc: The inhabitants of Earth leap into action to repel an impending invasion by a seemingly invincible Martian army, which possesses superior weaponry, a thirst for destruction and a wicked sense of humour. Tim Burton's sci-fi comedy, starring Jack Nicholson, Glenn Close, Pierce Brosnan, Annette Bening, Natalie Portman, Danny DeVito and Tom Jones

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice (2024)
programme.desc: A family tragedy woos Delia Deetz, daughter Lydia and rebellious teenage granddaughter Astrid back to the close-knit community of Winter River. Astrid accidentally opens a portal to the afterlife by summoning Beetlejuice three times. The mischievous demon ricochets between realms, wreaking havoc wherever he goes. Tim Burton's comedy sequel, starring Michael Keaton, Winona Ryder and Jenna Ortega

programme.title: This Is Spinal Tap (1984)
programme.desc: An ageing British rock band try to revive their fortunes by embarking on a US tour, with a film-maker on hand to record the mayhem. However, the big comeback soon turns into a series of disasters as tensions between the clueless rockers and their long-suffering manager reach breaking point. Spoof documentary, starring Michael McKean, Christopher Guest, Harry Shearer and Rob Reiner

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: 28 Days Later (2002)
programme.desc: A man wakes from a coma to discover Britain has been struck by a terrifying experimental virus that sends victims into a homicidal rage. With a handful of other survivors, he heads for Manchester, the last outpost unaffected by the outbreak - but realises the virus is not the only threat. Danny Boyle's horror, starring Cillian Murphy, Naomie Harris, Brendan Gleeson and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: The Man with Two Brains (1983)
programme.desc: A brilliant surgeon escapes the heartache and frustration of his failing marriage by falling in love with a bottled human brain - but needs to find a suitable body to implant it in. Sci-fi comedy, starring Steve Martin, Kathleen Turner and David Warner, with Sissy Spacek providing the voice of the doctor's beloved brain

programme.title: National Lampoon's Vacation (1983)
programme.desc: A bumbling all-American family man takes his wife and kids on a disaster-filled cross-country drive to California to visit famous theme park Walley World - but their chances of arriving at their destination in one piece are looking slim. Comedy, starring Chevy Chase, Beverly D'Angelo, Randy Quaid, Imogene Coca, Anthony Michael Hall, Christie Brinkley and John Candy

programme.title: The 40 Year-Old Virgin (2005)
programme.desc: A middle-aged shop worker reveals he has never been intimate with a woman, which prompts his shocked colleagues to set him up with a series of potentially passionate dates. When he falls for a single mother, his friends think he is right on course for a lusty liaison, but his new partner is not ready for a physical relationship. Judd Apatow's comedy, starring Steve Carell, Catherine Keener and Paul Rudd

programme.title: The 4:30 Movie (2024)
programme.desc: During the 1980s, New Jersey teenager Brian David and his friends sneak into different movie theatres. Writer/director Kevin Smith's semi-autobiographical teen comedy, starring Austin Zajur, Nicholas Cirillo and Reed Northrup

programme.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles (1987)
programme.desc: A stressed businessman just wants to make it home to spend Thanksgiving with his family. However, when his flight is forced to land miles from its destination, he finds himself sharing a disaster-strewn journey across America with an amiable but slobbish salesman. Comedy road movie, starring Steve Martin, John Candy, Laila Robins, Kevin Bacon and Michael McKean

Tim Burton's sci-fi comedy, starring Jack Nicholson, Glenn Close, Pierce Brosnan, Annette Bening, Natalie Portman, Danny DeVito and Tom Jones

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)

Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: The Phoenician Scheme (2025)
programme.desc: Wealthy businessman Zsa-Zsa Korda appoints his only daughter, a nun, as sole heir to his estate. As Korda embarks on a new enterprise, they become the target of scheming tycoons, foreign terrorists and determined assassins. Writer/director Wes Anderson's comedy, starring Benicio del Toro, Mia Threapleton and Michael Cera

programme.title: Mickey 17 (2025)
programme.desc: In 2054, criminal Mickey Barnes signs up to be a worker who agrees to be reprinted every time he perishes. He flees Earth with friend and accomplice Timo on an interstellar mission to the remote colony of Niflheim, masterminded by Kenneth Marshall and his wife, Ylfa. The 17th iteration of Mickey is presumed dead after a close encounter with creatures on the planet's surface, but is saved from a grim fate, and when he arrives back at base, Mickey meets his reprinted self. Director Bong Joon Ho's sci-fi comedy, starring Robert Pattinson, Naomi Ackie, Mark Ruffalo, Toni Collette and Steven Yeun

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes (2009)
programme.desc: The eccentric detective and his confidant Dr Watson investigate the apparent resurrection of a criminal mastermind with links to the occult, who is carrying out a series of seemingly magical murders. The case is complicated by the reappearance of Holmes' duplicitous old flame Irene Adler. Action thriller, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Rachel McAdams, Mark Strong and Eddie Marsan

programme.title: 28 Years Later (2025)
programme.desc: Decades since the rage virus took hold, the survivors live on a small island. When one of the group leaves on a mission into the mainland, he discovers secrets, wonders, and horrors that have mutated not only the infected but other survivors. Danny Boyle's horror thriller sequel, starring Jodie Comer, Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Ralph Fiennes and Rocco Haynes

programme.title: The 4:30 Movie (2024)
programme.desc: During the 1980s, New Jersey teenager Brian David and his friends sneak into different movie theatres. Writer/director Kevin Smith's semi-autobiographical teen comedy, starring Austin Zajur, Nicholas Cirillo and Reed Northrup

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Cameron agrees to attend a week-long boot camp run by retiring star quarterback Isaiah White at his secluded desert compound. However, Cade's life soon turns into a nightmare. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

programme.title: The Cinema List: Race Against Time
programme.desc: This edition is about the race against time. From a deadly threat in Fuze to The Desperate Hour, where Naomi Watts faces every parent's nightmare - the clock is ticking

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: We Bury the Dead (2024)
programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.title: The Plague (2025)
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp as his anxiety spirals into psychological turmoil over the summer. Thriller, starring Joel Edgerton and Everett Blunck

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: One Last Deal (2026)
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent scrambles to land one last high-stakes deal for his star client, with both their futures on the line. Drama, starring Danny Dyer and Elliott Rogers

programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Cameron agrees to attend a week-long boot camp run by retiring star quarterback Isaiah White at his secluded desert compound. However, Cade's life soon turns into a nightmare. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

programme.title: The Plague (2025)
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp as his anxiety spirals into psychological turmoil over the summer. Thriller, starring Joel Edgerton and Everett Blunck

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.desc: Amy Carlson's relatives reveal her past, while her followers detail Jason Costilo's ascension to Father God - and their leader's declining health

programme.title: Black Widow
programme.desc: Documentary following the decade-long police investigation into Dena Thompson, who carried out a sustained campaign of fraud, deception, bigamy and murder before she was finally caught and sent to prison in 2003. Following her release in 2022, her victims, as well as the police officers involved in the case, decided to tell their tales about one of the worst cases of abuse and coercive control by a woman in British history

programme.title: Juan Carlos: Downfall of the King
programme.desc: The King's ex-lover Corinna zu Sayn-Wittgenstein becomes a threat to the monarchy, and an operation is launched to stop sensitive files going public

programme.title: Bruno v Tyson
programme.desc: The story of the boxing rivalry between Frank Bruno and Mike Tyson, spanning two epic fights marred by politics and volatile race relations, with the old foes coming together for a reunion

programme.title: Everton: Howard's Way (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary film telling the story of Everton and how they reached their their trophy-winning peak in the 1980s under the stewardship of Howard Kendall

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of writer and film-maker Nora Ephron, who was nominated for Academy Awards for her screenplays for Silkwood, When Harry Met Sally and Sleepless in Seattle

programme.title: Black Widow
programme.desc: As Lee Wyatt recognises that he is a victim of his wife's coercive control, police believe he could be the key to solving the mystery of Julian Webb's death

programme.title: Murder at the Post Office
programme.desc: In 2010, the North Yorkshire village of Melsonby was left in shock when postmistress Diana Garbutt was found murdered in the post office she ran with her husband Robin. He claimed she'd been the victim of a violent intruder, but as detectives probed further, suspicion began to fall on him and he was convicted of her murder and sentenced to life in prison in 2011. This documentary re-examines the case, featuring interviews with those convinced Robin was wrongfully convicted and others who are unsure and still suspect he is guilty

programme.title: Murder at the Post Office
programme.desc: At trial, the prosecution alleges Robin was stealing from the Post Office and evidence from the Horizon computer system is a key part of the case against him

programme.title: Murder at the Post Office
programme.desc: Robin is sentenced to 20 years, but key evidence is missing, with none of his DNA on the murder weapon and data from the Horizon system unable be trusted

programme.title: Salt Path: A Very British Scandal
programme.desc: A look at Observer journalist Chloe Hadjimatheou's investigation into allegations of factual inaccuracies in Raynor Winn's book The Salt Path

programme.title: MoviePass, MovieCrash
programme.desc: The story of MoviePass, the company that offered cinema tickets at a discounted rate. It was a movie lover's dreaam, but in just eight years it went from being the fastest-growing subscription service since Spotify to total bankruptcy. This documentary chronicles the visionary mission of its co-founders, its early success and how it finally failed due to mismanagement and corporate greed

programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary about French street performer Philippe Petit. Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge

programme.title: When We Were Kings (1996)
programme.desc: Leon Gast's Oscar-winning documentary charting the challenge of an ageing Muhammad Ali for George Foreman's World Heavyweight title held in Zaire in 1974. The film explores the impact of Ali's visit to Africa before focusing on the epic fight, dubbed the 'Rumble in the Jungle', which has gone down in history as one of the greatest of all time. Spike Lee and Norman Mailer are among the contributors offering personal opinions on its importance to them

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The murders of 16 people in Hungerford, west Berkshire, in August 1987, when unemployed labourer and antiques dealer Michael Ryan went on a shooting spree. programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts finds out about discoveries that shed light on the Tudor Age, visiting excavations at Shakespeare's first theatre in London's Shoreditch and at his last home in Stratford-upon-Avon, where clues reveal his economical use of money. In Wales, she meets a team of archaeologists learning about the realities of Henry VIII's dissolution of the monasteries, while the history of a forgotten royal palace is uncovered on the banks of the Thames

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. In a Roman-themed first edition, she examines the mystery surrounding the skeletons of 97 babies found beside the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon and a cult figure buried for 1,700 years beneath a fort

programme.title: Apocalypse D-Day
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. A titanic fleet took on the beaches of Normandy, intent on liberating Europe from Hitler's Nazis. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.title: Apocalypse D-Day
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. A titanic fleet took on the beaches of Normandy, intent on liberating Europe from Hitler's Nazis. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of moving testimonies and spectacular colourised archives

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. This second of two programmes concludes events, considered day by day, up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of moving testimonies and spectacular colourised archives

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Lost U-Boats of WWII
programme.desc: During the Second World War, the Nazis looted an estimated 600 tons of gold, thousands of pieces of artwork, and millions of priceless artifacts. While some of these items have been found, much of it remains missing. Thanks to top-secret documents acquired over 40 years of research, treasure hunter Darrell Miklos believes some of these stolen riches were loaded into specially modified U-Boats that are currently lying at the bottom of the Caribbean Sea. He and his team of divers and researchers set out to find these lost vessels

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee concludes his two-part exploration of the feline family tree, revealing how the Canadian lynx learned to thrive in the snow and spread out, evolving and inhabiting new parts of the Americas. In Belize, he mounts an experiment that showcases the margay's supersharp senses and gravity-defying acrobatics, and heads to the training track with a cheetah to find out what speed it is capable of. Finally, Patrick tells the story of the cat's domestication in Essaouira, Morocco, believed to be one of the first places where one particular species first gave up life in the wild in return for food, warmth, security and a home

As Tyreese and Carol grow attached to the shelter, they face growing problems with Lizzie's troubling behaviour. Post-apocalyptic drama, starring Chad L Coleman

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: In the penultimate episode, Phelan tries to right his wrongs, and Divis is given one last chance to try and prove himself

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and the team explore the Edinburgh Vaults, a series of underground tunnels that used to house hundreds of people

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and the team visit Leith Hall in Aberdeenshire, a manor house rumoured to have a haunted music room

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and the team explore Mary King's Close, a 17th-century building in Edinburgh reported to house the spirits of victims of the plague. A child's head, a severed arm and a girl in a blue dress are among the spooky phenomena to have been spotted there

programme.title: You Can Live Forever (2022)
programme.desc: After moving into a Jehovah's Witness community, lesbian teenager Jamie begins a life-changing love affair with the daughter of a prominent Witness Elder. Drama, starring Anwen O'Driscoll and June Laporte

programme.title: Rurangi (2020)
programme.desc: After leaving a decade earlier, a transgender activist returns to the remote community of Rurangi, hoping to reconnect with his father. Drama, starring Elz Carrad and Arlo Green

programme.title: Cicada (2020)
programme.desc: As they continue to recover from separate traumatic events, two young men gradually start to fall for each other. Drama, starring Matthew Fifer and Sheldon D Brown

programme.title: Summerland (2020)
programme.desc: Alice is a reclusive writer, resigned to a solitary life on the seaside cliffs of southern England while the Second World War rages across the Channel. When she opens her front door one day to find she has to adopt a young London evacuee named Frank, she's resistant. It's not long, however, before the two realise they have more in common in their pasts than Alice had assumed. Romantic drama, starring Gemma Arterton, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Tom Courtenay

programme.title: Mascarpone (2021)
programme.desc: A young gay man who must carve out a new path for himself after his husband - who he depends on financially - dumps him. Italian comedy drama, starring Giancarlo Commare and Eduardo Valdarnini

programme.title: Minyan (2020)
programme.desc: New York City during the 1980s, and a young gay Russian immigrant looks to escape the constraints of his community and find a world teeming with possibilities. Drama, starring Samuel H Levine and Ron Rifkin

programme.title: Am I OK? (2022)
programme.desc: Lifelong pals Lucy and Jane face a test of friendship when one of them embarks on a personal journey. Romantic comedy drama, starring Dakota Johnson, Sonoya Mizuno and Jermaine Fowler

programme.title: Plainclothes (2025)
programme.desc: An undercover agent assigned to lure and arrest gay men defies orders when he falls for a target. Writer/director Carmen Emmi's thriller, starring Tom Blyth, Russell Tovey and Maria Dizzia

programme.title: Chuck Chuck Baby (2024)
programme.desc: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby, and his dying mother Gwen. Helen's life is a grind, but she has a chance at romance when a secret love returns. Writer/director Janis Pugh's drama, starring Louise Brealey and Annabel Scholey

programme.title: A Nice Indian Boy (2024)
programme.desc: Naveen brings his fiancé Jay home to meet his traditional Indian family, and they come to terms with his white-orphan-artist boyfriend while planning the wedding of their dreams. Romantic comedy, starring Karan Soni and Jonathan Groff

programme.title: The Wedding Banquet (2025)
programme.desc: A gay man makes a deal with his lesbian friend - a green-card marriage for him, in exchange for in vitro fertilisation treatments for her. Romantic comedy, starring Bowen Yang, Lily Gladstone and Kelly Marie Tran

programme.title: Wicked (2024)
programme.desc: Green-skinned Elphaba Thropp becomes part of the new intake at Shiz University, and shares a room with fellow spell-caster Galinda Upland. It's the beginning of a friendship which culminates in an epic showdown as Elphaba transforms a renegade witch with a skill for bursting into show-stopping songs, like Defying Gravity. Part one of The Wizard of Oz prequel based on the hit stage musical, starring Cynthia Erivo, Ariana Grande-Butera, Jonathan Bailey, Michelle Yeoh and Jeff Goldblum

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Bonus Track (2023)
programme.desc: An aspiring teenage musician teams up with the son of a rock star to try and win the school talent show. Comedy, starring Joe Anders, Samuel Small and Susan Wokoma

programme.title: Mascarpone (2021)
programme.desc: A young gay man who must carve out a new path for himself after his husband - who he depends on financially - dumps him. Italian comedy drama, starring Giancarlo Commare and Eduardo Valdarnini

programme.title: The Man with the Answers (2021)
programme.desc: A former diving champion and an eccentric student take an adventurous road trip of rediscovery. Romantic drama, starring Vasilis Magouliotis, Anton Weil and Stella Fyrogeni. In Greek, Italian, German and English

programme.title: Beach Rats (2017)
programme.desc: Powerful drama about a troubled Brooklyn teen aimlessly drifting through summer while struggling to come to terms with his own desires. Starring Harris Dickinson

programme.title: You Can Live Forever (2022)
programme.desc: After moving into a Jehovah's Witness community, lesbian teenager Jamie begins a life-changing love affair with the daughter of a prominent Witness Elder. Drama, starring Anwen O'Driscoll and June Laporte

programme.title: The United States Vs. Billie Holiday (2021)
programme.desc: The acclaimed singer is targeted by the Federal Department of Narcotics with an undercover sting operation led by Federal Agent Jimmy Fletcher, with who she had a tumultuous affair. Director Lee Daniels' biopic based on the novel by Johann Hari, starring Andra Day, Trevante Rhodes and Natasha Lyonne

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Monsoon (2019)
programme.desc: A British-Vietnamese man returns to his birth country for the first time in more than 30 years. He was only eight when he and his family escaped Saigon as boat refugees during the Vietnam-American war. Now he embarks on a personal journey from Saigon to Hanoi to find a place to scatter his parents' ashes. Drama, starring Henry Golding, Parker Sawyers, David Tran and Molly Harris

programme.title: Wicked (2024)
programme.desc: Green-skinned Elphaba Thropp becomes part of the new intake at Shiz University, and shares a room with fellow spell-caster Galinda Upland. It's the beginning of a friendship which culminates in an epic showdown as Elphaba transforms a renegade witch with a skill for bursting into show-stopping songs, like Defying Gravity. Part one of The Wizard of Oz prequel based on the hit stage musical, starring Cynthia Erivo, Ariana Grande-Butera, Jonathan Bailey, Michelle Yeoh and Jeff Goldblum

programme.title: Blue Moon (2025)
programme.desc: Set over the course of one turbulent night in March 1943, Lorenz Hart, the waspish wordsmith responsible for The Lady Is a Tramp and My Funny Valentine, finds his life unravelling on the opening evening of his ex-partner's show, Oklahoma! Director Richard Linklater's biographical comedy drama, starring Ethan Hawke, Margaret Qualley, Bobby Cannavale and Andrew Scott

programme.title: On Swift Horses (2025)
programme.desc: Mild-mannered war veteran Lee Walker plans a life of quiet domesticity in California with his wife Muriel and younger brother Julius. Restless after an early discharge from the US Army, Julius steals money from Lee to seek his fortune in Nevada, where he lands a job in security at a casino and sparks forbidden desire with co-worker Henry, which is consummated away from prying eyes. Back in their hotel room, Julius and Henry fantasise about using the same underhand tactics as card sharps to line their pockets. Drama, starring Daisy Edgar-Jones, Jacob Elordi and Will Poulter

programme.title: Rocketman (2019)
programme.desc: Elton John enters an addiction rehabilitation session, and recounts his life in a flashback, covering his days as young Reginald Dwight growing up in 1950s Britain. He takes piano lessons, makes his way into the Royal Academy of Music, and hones his craft in local pubs before becoming one of the world's biggest pop stars. Golden Globe-winning biopic, starring Taron Egerton, Jamie Bell and Richard Madden

programme.title: Carmen & Lola (2018)
programme.desc: After two Roma women's lives collide in the suburbs of Madrid, they are forced to make a difficult choice between community and love. Award-winning LGBTQ+ drama, starring Rosy Rodríguez and Zaira Romero

programme.desc: A detective's search for a blackmailer is hampered by a succession of voluptuous women who cannot seem to resist his manly charms. Sex comedy, starring Christopher Neil, Suzy Kendall, Harry H Corbett, Irene Handl and Liz Fraser

programme.title: Frustrated Wives (1974)
programme.desc: A pair of frustrated housewives who are bored and neglected by their husbands decide to go to a sex clinic in the country. Contains nudity.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh shows how to create a fantasy garden complete with dressed archways, sculptures and winding pathways, and Charlotte Uhlenbroek demonstrates how to attract birds. Meanwhile, Matt James visits a reclamation yard in search of something magical for his plot

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh demonstrates how to create a flower garden, giving advice on the best way to achieve a blaze of colour and scent, and demonstrates the construction of a seated area with a sail shelter. Meanwhile, Valentine Warner makes chocolates with lavender, and Charlotte Uhlenbroek reveals which flowers are best for attracting butterflies

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Chris gets his hands on an inscribed Egyptian amulet and gives trainee auctioneer James a masterclass in ancient signs and symbols. A historical silver pin cushion, which was once owned by a leading lady of the Irish Arts and Crafts movement, piques the interest of competing buyers. Plus, auctioneer James gets up close and personal with the inner workings of some valuable watches

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Fine art dealer Gavin Claxton, market trader Gary Barton and aeronautical engineer and part-time upcycler Stu Abbott head to Durham to find some bargains they can turn into some flipping profit. As Stu gets mechanical, making money from bits and parts of a Mini, Gavin goes to auction to try to sell his pricey painting, and Gary uses all the Glasgow patter he can muster to sell his art deco bowls in the city's Barras market

programme.desc: Big Ben, bold beats, and secrets around every corner , London?s calling, and Pilar?s picking up. 3 days, zero rules

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: Van Pelt looks after a man who is willing to testify against a drug dealer, but her career is put in jeopardy when the witness is murdered. Jane learns the identity of the killer after questioning the victim's daughter, so the team rushes her to a safe house and waits for the hired gun to finish the job - only to be shocked by who appears. Crime drama, starring Amanda Righetti

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: When two hikers are discovered murdered near a remote coastal trail, the team is on the hunt for a fugitive duo wanted in a series of grisly campsite murders

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: When a bomb blast at a laboratory claims one seemingly innocent victim, Carrie must work out why she was targeted and stop the perpetrator striking again. The investigation uncovers a series of controversial experiments conducted by the deceased and leads to a suspect who is likely to be planning further bloodshed, but the case is turned upside down when it emerges he has been dead for five years

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Return to Paradise
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police officer Mackenzie Clarke is deployed to her Australian home town of Dolphin Cove after being accused of falsifying evidence. Once there, she faces scorn from her old acquaintances due to her earlier abandoning her fiancé at the altar. When a real estate salesman is found stabbed to death on a beach during a surfing competition, Mackenzie has to swallow her pride and work with her jilted former love, a forensic pathologist, and her other old colleagues to solve this perplexing crime. Death in Paradise spin-off, starring Anna Samson and Lloyd Griffith

programme.title: Allegiance
programme.desc: Star rookie police officer Sabrina Sohal must grapple with the limits of the justice system as she serves her diverse hometown. On Sabrina's first day with Serious Crimes, an investigation into a woman's murder leads her into the sinister side of the tech world as she adjusts to a more dangerous role

programme.title: The Hunting Party
programme.desc: Drama series following a former FBI profiler and her team as they track down serial killers who have escaped from a top-secret facility. After an explosion at an off-grid prison, a number of inmates go missing, including notorious serial killer Richard Harris. The government enlists former FBI profiler Bex Henderson to find him

programme.desc: Morgan has become obsessed with ruining Carrie, but when he is found dead, the detective finds herself under suspicion of murder. programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch investigates the death of a man found in a burned-out truck and catches a glimpse of the impossible - a long-gone nemesis back from the dead

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A man mysteriously appears after a bright flash of light in the middle of a field and claims to have been abducted by aliens

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The team investigates when a struggling gold prospector is killed, but Lisbon is injured during the opening stages of the case and Hightower takes her place. The detectives speak to the man's family and become suspicious when they discover his girlfriend had bought a one-way ticket to San Francisco a week earlier. Simon Baker, Robin Tunney and Aunjanue Ellis star

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: The team uncovers distorted loyalties that form when personal ties meet the trappings of fame when a troubled young pop star is suspected of killing her fiance

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Morgan has become obsessed with ruining Carrie, but when he is found dead, the detective finds herself under suspicion of murder. With a motive, incriminating fingerprints, and CCTV footage, her career is jeopardised, forcing her to push her remarkable memory to the limit to prove she has been set up

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The president of the local cricket club is found shot dead in the middle of the pitch after a recent victory. Suspicion falls on his family and fellow players, but all of them have an alibi. JP thinks Dwayne is keeping something from him and wonders how well he knows his friend, and Humphrey dreads Martha's imminent departure from the island

programme.title: Saint-Pierre
programme.desc: Arch and Fitz race against time to save a kidnapped flight attendant, and things get tense when one of their own is kidnapped next

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Bunty reunites with Father Brown to uncover a dark secret and mysterious goings-on at the local girls' borstal. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams and Emer Kenny

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Customs and Border Protection officers are stunned when they discover more than $2million of cocaine mixed in with gallons of household paint and bottles of beer

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Vigilant Customs and Border Protection officers uncover 5,000 rounds of ammunition being smuggled across Hidalgo Bridge into Mexico

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the home of New Nordic cuisine - Copenhagen - where he goes foraging for rosehip and learns about the art of pickling buds, bark and flowers. He's cheered to find plenty of traditional favourites, such as the city's famous Smorrebrod open sandwiches on rye bread, delicious poached turbot with cabbage and lemon butter sauce and - of course - the ubiquitous Danish pastry. Back at home, Rick cooks fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce, which has been voted Denmark's favourite dish

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May goes for a spin in the Subaru Legacy, Richard Hammond tests Peugeot and Renault's coupe convertibles and Jeremy Clarkson looks at the changing face of Bentley with the Continental GT. Comic actor Rob Brydon is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and the team attempt to find out the safest way to escape from a submerged vehicle

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Lamborghini Gallardo through its paces, Richard Hammond tests enduring classic the Miura, and James May gets behind the wheel of 1980s favourite the Countach. Plus, a series of tuned Mini Coopers are taken for a spin, and the team stages a unique car long jump competition. American comedian Rich Hall is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for barbel in Hay-on-Wye, and sample the great outdoors in spectacular fashion when Mortimer decides to strip in celebration of their catch. Passing a graveyard, they muse about the future and chat to a local vicar about death and their own funerals. To lighten the mood, back in their wooden fishing cabin, Bob promises Paul a very special treat if they are successful with their angling efforts

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the impact of humans on the Lake District, discovering that the high number of visitors to Scafell Pike is damaging England's highest peak and stretching the resources of mountain rescue volunteers. He goes wild swimming in the crystal clear lake of Buttermere, before taking a look at the less-fortunate Derwent Water, one of the many lakes now clogged with invasive New Zealand pygmyweed

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers focus on British sheep, which have provided the nation with milk, meat and wool for thousands of years. They rustle up a hogget and barley stew, lamb chops with kidneys, and North African dish mechoui lamb, and explore the history of the hardy breed found on North Ronaldsay in the Orkney Islands

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: It's a fiery finale as the teams race to rescue the hapless Donna from a burning flat. With a blaze to put out and puzzles to ponder, they must keep calm as the heat rises

programme.desc: Guests Phill Jupitus, Holly Walsh and Nish Kumar join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'nonsense' in an extended episode. programme.desc: Richard Osman, Ellie Taylor, Jessica Knappett, David O'Doherty, Rob Beckett and Aisling Bea answer questions based on opinion polls carried out around the country to see who best understands what is going on in Britain today. Jimmy Carr hosts

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Sukh Ojla and Mark Simmons. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A new recruit fresh out of training school makes her mark when she uncovers 12kg of cocaine hidden in a car that she has stopped on one of the bridges

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Customs and Border Protection officers think they have spotted a trend when they seize drugs from behind the dashboards of two cars. A Mexican woman asks officers for protection

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Cadiz in Spain, where he samples two of the city's most revered culinary stars - fresh tuna and Manzanilla sherry. Rick is captivated by the narrow winding streets as he walks in the footsteps of the ancient Phoenician and Arab traders, whose influence on the city can still be seen to this day. Back home, he prepares flamenco eggs and arroz verde, or green rice, made with garlic, parsley, clams and prawns, producing the vibrant colours of the Spanish flag

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests Toyota pick-up the Hilux with a series of extraordinary tasks, and puts the pedal to the metal with Mazda's sports coupe, the Wankel rotary-engined RX8. Meanwhile, James May puts the Fiat Panda through its paces, and Richard Hammond checks out classic cars in waiting - the Mercedes 190E and the VW Corrado. Simon Cowell is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson reviews Citroen's C2, while Richard Hammond compares convertible models of the Renault Megane and Peugeot 307, and James May looks back at Aston Martin's V8 Vantage. The team also tries to destroy a Toyota Hilux, and Sanjeev Bhaskar is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. In the Monsal Valley in Derbyshire, Paul tries to teach Bob how to fly fish for wild rainbow trout, and the pair reflect on their humble beginnings, their careers and their comedy contemporaries. Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the wild and rugged coast of Cumbria and learns how central the county is to many of the challenges facing Britain. He visits Sellafield, which is home to hundreds of ageing and disused nuclear facilities and meets people tasked with clearing and securing more than half a century of Britain's nuclear waste

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers revisit the 1970s, a decade which is all too often unfairly stigmatised for its food. They show how a well-prepared chicken cordon bleu and banoffee pie can be a contemporary gastronomic delight, before heading to a restaurant whose menu harks back to that era

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. programme.desc: A new recruit fresh out of training school makes her mark when she uncovers 12kg of cocaine hidden in a car that she has stopped on one of the bridges

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Customs and Border Protection officers think they have spotted a trend when they seize drugs from behind the dashboards of two cars. A Mexican woman asks officers for protection

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Cadiz in Spain, where he samples two of the city's most revered culinary stars - fresh tuna and Manzanilla sherry. Rick is captivated by the narrow winding streets as he walks in the footsteps of the ancient Phoenician and Arab traders, whose influence on the city can still be seen to this day. Back home, he prepares flamenco eggs and arroz verde, or green rice, made with garlic, parsley, clams and prawns, producing the vibrant colours of the Spanish flag

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests Toyota pick-up the Hilux with a series of extraordinary tasks, and puts the pedal to the metal with Mazda's sports coupe, the Wankel rotary-engined RX8. Meanwhile, James May puts the Fiat Panda through its paces, and Richard Hammond checks out classic cars in waiting - the Mercedes 190E and the VW Corrado. Simon Cowell is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson reviews Citroen's C2, while Richard Hammond compares convertible models of the Renault Megane and Peugeot 307, and James May looks back at Aston Martin's V8 Vantage. The team also tries to destroy a Toyota Hilux, and Sanjeev Bhaskar is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. In the Monsal Valley in Derbyshire, Paul tries to teach Bob how to fly fish for wild rainbow trout, and the pair reflect on their humble beginnings, their careers and their comedy contemporaries. Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the wild and rugged coast of Cumbria and learns how central the county is to many of the challenges facing Britain. He visits Sellafield, which is home to hundreds of ageing and disused nuclear facilities and meets people tasked with clearing and securing more than half a century of Britain's nuclear waste

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers revisit the 1970s, a decade which is all too often unfairly stigmatised for its food. programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo. They are on their way to meet experts who have discovered ancient cave art thought to be among the oldest on the planet. But they have also heard rumours that the mountain range contains further unmapped caves - and Steve wants to be the first to track them down, and whatever is hidden within

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to explore the Arctic at the most volatile and dangerous time of the year - the spring melt. Paddling through ever-shifting sea ice and kayaking the world's largest fjord, he and his team are on constant alert for giant icebergs and hungry polar bears

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve travels deep into Yucatán, Mexico, on the trail of the ancient Maya. His team, including Mexican archaeologist Guillermo de Andaa, pioneering cave diver Robbie Schmittner and former Royal Marine Aldo Kane, want to explore secret caves known as 'the place of fear'

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville invests in a giant balloon for advertising purposes, but the promotion does not goes as planned. He also has to come up with the best sales pitch he can muster to persuade his customers to buy tins of smoked oysters. Leroy comes under pressure from Beth to find a suitable man for Ruby

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright has a rival for Nurse Gladys Emmanuel's attention, as a visitor from Australia - and an old flame of hers- returns to his old stomping ground

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and Daisy go on a skiing trip to Eastern Europe, but as usual disaster isn't far behind. The trio of friends find themselves stuck in a cable car with a handful of other nervous tourists - one of whom just happens to be pregnant. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee gets jealous when Lucy talks about her first love, so Daisy helps him turn the tables by setting him up with her gorgeous friend. However, he is surprised when things start moving very quickly between them. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Bobin (Secret Diary of a Call Girl), with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville invests in a giant balloon for advertising purposes, but the promotion does not goes as planned. He also has to come up with the best sales pitch he can muster to persuade his customers to buy tins of smoked oysters. Leroy comes under pressure from Beth to find a suitable man for Ruby

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville has to deal with a malfunctioning till, while Eric and Cyril decide it is time to show some macho spirit in the home of the Tour de Yorkshire and get back in the saddle. Meanwhile, Leroy tells Beth about his sneaky plan to find a blind date for Ruby, and Mr Newbold is sent on a mission by Mrs Featherstone to find out more about Mrs Rossi and whether she poses a threat to the local shop. But does he discover more than he bargained for?

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and Daisy go on a skiing trip to Eastern Europe, but as usual disaster isn't far behind. The trio of friends find themselves stuck in a cable car with a handful of other nervous tourists - one of whom just happens to be pregnant. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out

However, he is surprised when things start moving very quickly between them. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Bobin (Secret Diary of a Call Girl), with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin the Teenager falls ill and Tim Nice-but-Dim finds a kindred spirit when he meets Dick Nice-but-Thick. Comedy, with Kathy Burke and Paul Whitehouse

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The spooks' thoughtless behaviour sends Alison into meltdown, prompting them to come up with a creative way to get back into her good books and show her how truly sorry they are. Mary however, believing she has done nothing wrong, refuses to take any responsibility, not after the fiery fate that befell her the last time she took the blame for something she didn't do. Alison and Mike have a last-minute cancellation for the guesthouse and decide that a relaxing day off is exactly what they need. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A shocking revelation throws the Ghosts into disarray. Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - children's birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee wins £1,000 for writing an essay entitled Disability in the Workplace, and Lucy is moved to tears by his sensitivity. However, Tim remains unconvinced, and his scepticism pays off when the journalist who turns up to cover the story is shocked to find an able-bodied author. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Tim Vine

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy announces to the friends that she is pregnant, causing Lee to wonder if he is the father. Comedy, starring Lee Mack as a man with a complicated relationship with his flatmate. With Sally Bretton, Tim Vine and Miranda Hart

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin the Teenager falls ill and Tim Nice-But-Dim meets a kindred spirit in Dick Nice-But-Thick. Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher helps cellmate Godber make it through the night, calling on his unpredictable and limitless imagination to while away the hours. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mary walks Julian through her journey of self-discovery and how she finally found her voice. Thomas realises enough is enough - he must go 'cold turkey' on his Alison addiction and needs Pat's undivided attention. Old friends of Mike check in to the guesthouse and agree to share the surprising secret of their success. The Captain is thrown by a change to his daily routine, so Kitty steps in to teach him how to live in the moment. Robin helps Lady Button to remember lost friends

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates face a day of hard labour digging drains on a lonely stretch of moorland. However, Fletcher soon finds a way of turning the situation to his advantage. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.desc: Edina is horrified when Bubble tells her she is about to be visited by Penny Caspar-Morse, an ultra-thin model she knew in the 1960s who used to torment her about her weight. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Fegg Hayes in Stoke-on-Trent, where Sue's family have lived for six generations. The DIY SOS team are determined to help get the job done, accompanied by hundreds of local tradespeople - only to run into the worst storms to hit Britain in recent years

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing stars Anton Du Beke, Katya Jones and Graziano Di Prima join Nick Knowles and the team in Wallsend, Newcastle. programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Beloved fluff monster Kylo, a 10-month-old rough collie, and golden retriever pup Sasha make return visits to the practice. programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 10-year-old Dobermann is treated for an aggressive bone tumour in its leg. One option is a full limb amputation, but another could potentially save Rolo's leg by cutting out the affected part of the bone and replacing it with a unique implant of Noel's own invention - a scapula-humeral endoprosthesis. Five-year-old labrador Jeff is suffering from developmental elbow disease, which cannot be managed by medication alone. The day of his planned surgery for a full elbow replacement arrives, though bad news places the prospect of such treatment in jeopardy

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Essex, where local mental health charity Butterfly Effect Well-being is in desperate need of a new meeting place. They have an army of local volunteer trades - and the cast of EastEnders, who have made the trip from Walford to help transform a derelict building into a warm and welcoming space for the local community

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team enlist the help of a local community in Aberystwyth, Ceredigion, to renovate the home of a builder with a brain tumour. They finish the construction of an extension to the man's home, which had been left unfinished owing to his ill health, and call on the skills of his friends and family to complete the job

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Staffy Boxer Cross Crumble, who's been at the centre in Basildon for over three years, stuck without a foster or permanent home in sight

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.desc: Jennifer Lopez stars in this crime drama about a Brooklyn detective and single mother who is forced to turn informant on her crooked team, led by a corrupt NYPD Lieutenant (Ray Liotta). Harlee Santos, a detective from a tight-knit group of Brooklyn cops with questionable tactics, is arrested by an FBI agent who wants her to be an informant

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.desc: Harlee struggles under the pressure of acting as an informant on her crew - when Wozniak worries about a mole, she suggests putting everyone through a lie detector test

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Danny Dyer and daughter Dani join Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell on an antiques hunt in Essex that features pirates, a raft race, sunken treasure and a Chinese banquet

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors Robert Daws and Mark Curry go antique hunting in Lancashire, where they find a real-life Sherlock Holmes and join a drag queen cabaret. Roo Irvine and Izzy Balmer offer expert advice

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy hits the headlines in a sleazy tabloid sex scandal, while melodramatic Edina demands hospital treatment for a trivial complaint. Comedy, with guest stars Mandy Rice-Davies, Helena Bonham Carter, Richard E Grant and Germaine Greer

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina becomes obsessed by mortality following her father's death and sets about assembling an art collection as a tribute to herself. Unfortunately, while she's buying pieces, a mix-up over where the dearly departed is being stored leads her to make an exhibition of him. Acclaimed comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley, June Whitfield and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: The jet-set duo head for sunny Morocco where, after selling Saffy to slave traders, they learn a Vogue fashion shoot is a little too close for comfort. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Derek gets a tour of Hadlow Castle in Kent when he arrives to collect the owner's pre-war Alvis and an immaculate Citroen used by the Gestapo in occupied France

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek auctions off some vehicles that were used in the TV show Heartbeat. There's love for an Austin Cambridge and a sporty Italian number finds a new home

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: In 1925 the SS Cotopaxi carrying coal and 32 men vanished. It has since become synonymous with one of the ocean's greatest mysteries - the Bermuda Triangle

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons sell a 1958 Bedford D122 and a 1969 Vanden Plas Princess. They also meet a collector who may be willing to finally part with his 220-car collection

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There is a triple collection of desirable cars in store for Derek when an MG Midget, an Austin-Healey 'frogeye' Sprite and an RS2000 come up for auction

programme.title: Tankies: Tank Heroes of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. BBC diplomatic editor and former army officer Mark Urban examines the exploits of six members of the 5th Royal Tank Regiment during the Second World War, beginning in northern France, where in 1940 rookie driver Gerry Solomon joined veterans including Jake Wardrop and Harry Finlayson. He follows in the soldiers' tracks across the deserts of North Africa, where he looks at the battles of Sidi Rezegh, Alam el Halfa and El Alamein, which changed the course of the conflict. With archive footage, including rarely seen colour film, as well as first-hand testimony from surviving veterans and letters and diaries

programme.title: Tankies: Tank Heroes of WW2
programme.desc: The two-part documentary telling the stories of six men who served in the Royal Tank Regiment concludes, with the men taking part in the D-Day landings, the battle for Normandy and the eventual victory in Germany

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents as the team assesses rarities at Leeds Town Hall. Among the items brought in by West Yorkshire residents are a diamond brooch left as security for an unpaid off-licence bill, a 15th-century spoon found by a builder digging a trench, and a single glass whose valuation leaves its owner lost for words

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second programme from Leeds Town Hall. The experts assess an enormous sculpture of carved elephants that weighs a ton and was bought online, a 16th-century gold ring dug up in a field, and a doll with a tragic tale about its background, which has not been removed from its glass case in 100 years
